title: Automotive Maintenance Operations & Routing Coordinator
description:
Key Responsibilities



1. Fleet & Retail Ops Coordination

*Own the estimate-to-approval-to-route workflow:

*Create and submit estimates

*Follow up for approval

*Ensure parts are sourced

*Finalize tech routes for the following day

*Track all jobs in progress and flag issues proactively

*Ensure routes are realistic and efficient; confirm techs are prepped and ready

*Communicate clearly with customers and fleet managers about job timing, status, and follow-ups

*Submit and close out fleet approvals in Element, LeasePlan, and AutoIntegrate

*Ensure invoices match approvals; follow up on outstanding invoices and missing payments



2. Scheduling & Productivity Tracking

*Maintain the daily technician schedule, making updates as needed

*Monitor tech productivity; flag idle time, reschedules, or delays

*Track time logs and coordinate with bookkeeper for payroll



3. Customer Communication

*Respond to incoming texts, WhatsApp messages, and system inquiries in a timely, professional manner

*Follow up with clients as needed to resolve open issues or gather job-related info

*Escalate concerns appropriately and promptly



4. Admin & Reporting

*Submit a detailed Daily Recap covering completed work, pending tasks, blockers, and KPIs

*Keep Trello and other tools up-to-date with accurate job and task statuses

*Track inventory needs and coordinate ordering of parts, oil, and supplies

*Monitor A/R and follow up on past-due fleet invoices



5. Marketing & Content Support

*Publish our monthly newsletter in MailChimp with content from marketing team

*Coordinate publishing of content to Instagram /
 / Google My Business

*Other light admin support as needed for the marketing team



What Success Looks Like

*All work orders, estimates, approvals, and tech routes are handled with zero dropped balls

*You identify blockers or problems before they become fires

*Communication is clear, timely, and professional — with both customers and internal team

*The business runs like a machine — nothing gets missed or delayed

*You take full ownership of your responsibilities and require very little follow-up



Who You Are

*A natural systems thinker who thrives on clarity, organization, and ownership

*Extremely proactive — you don’t wait to be told what to do

*Excellent at written and verbal English communication

*Comfortable in the automotive world and eager to learn the details

*Sharp and reliable — you retain info, spot gaps, and follow through



Experience & Skills Required

*3+ years of experience in a remote automotive: coordinator, dispatcher, operations role

*Demonstrated responsibility for keeping things moving without handholding

*Excellent written and spoken English

*Experience with dispatch, route planning, or technician scheduling

*Familiar with tools like Slack, Google Docs, Trello (or similar), CRM/invoicing platforms

*Experience working with U.S.-based field service, auto, or fleet companies

*Bonus: Familiarity with AutoIntegrate, Housecall Pro, Element, or LeasePlan platforms



Required Software Experience

*Slack

*WhatsApp

*Trello (or similar)

*Google Workspace

*CRM / invoicing tools (Housecall Pro, AutoIntegrate)

* Monday . com (plus)



TECH REQUIREMENTS:

*Computer - at least i5 or equivalent with 8gb RAM

*Internet Speed - At least 50 MBPS Download speed

*Dual monitor setup is required to ensure efficiency in managing multiple applications simultaneously.

*Location: Remote (Philippines)

*Schedule: Part-time or Full-time, US hours



*Part-time Schedule

**Tuesday 1-5 pm

**Wednesday 1-5 pm

**Friday 8:30 - 7:30 pm

**Saturday 10-4 pm

**Sunday 2-5 pm



*Full-time Schedule

**Monday to Friday 9:30AM -6:30PM

Pay: Php288.46 - Php432.69 hourly rate



Application Instructions:

Fill out this form: 
/appqNAvkEQ10YFlc3/pagrX4PvZe0xsKHWT/form

After submitting the form, click the apply button and send us a short message on why you would be a good fit for the role.

title: CRM Data Processing VA ((1 Full-Time | 1 Part-Time – Project-Based)
description:
We are hiring 2 Virtual Assistants ((1 Full-Time | 1 Part-Time – Project-Based) to handle retention corrections and data processing across our CRM systems.



This role requires high accuracy (98%+), strong attention to detail, and comfortable communicating with U.S.-based clients.



What You’ll Do

- Correct and update policy data between Salesforce and HawkSoft

-  Identify and resolve discrepancies in policy information

-  Process policy changes (rewrites, cancellations, payments, lost policies)

-  Accurately input and maintain sensitive account data

-  Pull and process renewal information

-  Update payment and policy statuses

- Support the client payment unit by calling clients regarding policy payments



Screening Questions:

1. What CRM systems have you used (Salesforce, HawkSoft, etc.) and what tasks did you handle?

2.  How do you ensure accuracy when doing repetitive data entry?

3.  Describe your experience handling data discrepancies or corrections.

4.  Are you comfortable speaking with U.S. clients? Briefly describe your experience.



How to apply:  

Send the following to 
 (Please indicate whether you are interested in working Full time or Part time)

1.  Resume

2.  Cover Letter

3.  Answers to the Screening Questions

4.  Typing test result (must show speed and at least 98?curacy)

You may use any typing test tool (e.g., 10fastfingers, 
). Please include a screenshot of your results.

title: Meta Ads Media Buyer (B2B Lead Gen – NO Creative Work)
description:
***DO NOT APPLY UNTIL YOU HAVE READ THIS WHOLE JOB POST***



We’re looking for a Meta Ads Media Buyer to manage and optimise B2B lead generation campaigns for service-based businesses.



This is an execution-focused role. You will NOT be responsible for creating ad creatives or building strategy from scratch. Instead, you’ll work within proven systems to test, optimise, and scale campaigns inside Ads Manager.



If you’re strong at reading data, making decisions, and improving performance — this role is for you.



What You’ll Be Doing



Managing Meta ad campaigns (primarily lead generation)

Testing new creatives (provided to you)

Turning off underperforming ads based on data

Scaling winning ads and ad sets

Monitoring CPL, lead quality, and overall performance

Making daily optimisation decisions inside Ads Manager

Communicating when new creatives are needed based on performance



What You WON’T Be Doing



Creating ad creatives

Writing copy or hooks

Building funnels or landing pages

Client communication



Requirements



Experience managing Meta 
/Instagram) ads

Strong understanding of lead generation campaigns (not ecom)

Experience with B2B campaigns

Ability to analyse performance and make clear decisions

Comfortable working with multiple ad accounts

Reliable, consistent, and detail-oriented

Good written communication



Nice to Have



Experience optimising for booked calls / qualified leads (not just cheap CPL)

Familiar with testing frameworks and scaling strategies



Compensation



On-target earnings: $1,000-$1,600/month

Opportunity to grow with more accounts over time



TO APPLY PLEASE ANSWER THESE SIX QUESTIONS:



1. Your experience with Meta ads (brief summary)

2. How much ad spend you have managed

3. Types of campaigns you’ve managed (lead gen, niches, etc.)

4. Any results you’ve achieved (CPL, volume, etc.)

5. How you d
 when to turn off an ad

6. Share a
 or Instagram ad you find highly effective and explain why it works.





We’re looking for someone long-term who can take ownership of execution and help us scale campaigns efficiently.

title: Executive Assistant to CEO
description:
Position Overview:



As the Executive Assistant to the CEO, you will play a pivotal role in ensuring the smooth and efficient functioning of the executive office. Your primary responsibility will be to provide comprehensive administrative support to the CEO, managing their schedule, and coordinating various tasks to enhance productivity. Your ability to maintain confidentiality, exercise sound judgment, and proactively handle administrative duties will contribute to the overall effectiveness of the CEO's office.





Pay Range: $8 per hour with a performance review scheduled after 90 days of employment. We believe in rewarding exceptional talents.





About Power Plus Systems:



Power Plus Systems LLC has been a dominant force in the residential electrical contracting industry for two decades, and we continue to lead the way. Our vision is to successfully wire 28,000 homes to completion by the year 2029, ensuring safe and efficient electrical solutions, thereby making a positive impact on the lives of our customers and communities. At Power Plus Systems, LLC, a position with us isn't just a job; it's an opportunity to grow and develop within a company that genuinely appreciates your contributions and dedication to our shared mission.





Objectives:

• Manage and organize the CEO's daily schedule, including meetings, appointments, and travel arrangements.

• Act as a gatekeeper, screening and prioritizing communications to the CEO.

• Coordinate and facilitate communication between the CEO and internal/external stakeholders.

• Anticipate the CEO's needs and proactively handle tasks to ensure optimal time management.

• Coordinate special projects and initiatives as assigned by the CEO.





Competencies:

• Exceptional organizational and multitasking skills, with keen attention to detail.

• Strong written and verbal communication skills.

• Ability to maintain confidentiality and exercise discretion in handling sensitive information.

• Proficient in using Microsoft Office and Google Suites.

• Familiarity with scheduling software; experience with additional productivity tools is an advantage.

• Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to interact effectively with individuals at all levels.

• Proactive problem solver and resourceful thinker.





Equipment Requirements:

• Must own a reliable computer or laptop capable of handling administrative tasks efficiently.

• Must own and actively use an iPhone and/or MacBook or Mac computer for efficient communication and task management.

• High-speed internet connection to support video calls, cloud-based collaboration, and remote work.





Physical Requirements:

• Extended periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer.



Benefits:

• Growth opportunities in a well-established company.

• Ongoing training and professional development opportunities.

• Opportunity for advancement within the company.

• A supportive and collaborative work environment.

• Company-observed paid United States holidays (full time).

• Paid vacation.





How to Apply:



If you are ready to join Power Plus Systems, LLC, a well-established residential electrical contractor that values its tea
mbers and offers excellent benefits, please submit your application through the link below.




/internationalcareers



Power Plus Systems, LLC is an equal opportunity employer and welcomes applicants from all backgrounds to apply.



Join us at Power Plus Systems, LLC, and be part of our continued success as we embark on the next 20 years of excellence in residential electrical contracting!

title: Senior Setter (Warm Leads|UK Hours)
description:
We are hiring a Senior Setter for Little Pixies Studios, a premium children’s portrait studio in the UK.



This is a phone sales role for someone who is excellent at building trust quickly, handling emotional conversations well, and confidently securing paid bookings from warm leads.



**This role is best suited to a mother with strong phone sales ability, because the conversations are emotionally led and centred around speaking with mums.**



This is not a customer support role and it is not a cold calling role.

You will be speaking to warm inbound leads and follow-up leads, helping them book our fairy tale photo shoot experience and secure their paid booking.



Pay

35,000 PHP base salary + Uncapped 5% commission



Realistic OTE: 60,000 PHP+ per month (Strong performers can earn more)



Schedule

Remote - Monday - Saturday UK hours



What you’ll be doing:

Calling warm leads quickly and confidently

Following up persistently until leads are booked or genuinely abandoned

Building rapport fast and making mums feel understood

Securing paid bookings and deposits over the phone

Managing your pipeline properly inside GoHighLevel

Sending follow-ups through our systems when needed

Giving honest feedback on lead quality, objections, and sales process issues

Taking ownership of results



Standards for this role

Minimum 15% close rate

Fast follow-up speed

5-minute call time on new leads

Consistent pipeline management

Strong follow-up discipline



You are probably a strong fit if:

You are a mother

You have at least 2 years of phone sales / telesales / transactional sales experience

You are excellent at building rapport and handling emotional sales conversations

You are highly fluent in English with trusted accent (e.g. American, British, Irish, South African)

You sound polished, clear, and trustworthy on the phone

You are confident asking for the sale

You are self-sufficient, organised, and performance-driven

You take feedback well and care about improving



Strong preference for people with experience in:

Luxury sales / Emotional sales (Photography sales, Fitness sales, Luxury vehicle, High-trust phone sales)

Any role where rapport, emotion, and follow-up mattered more than hard-pressure tactics

Clear, trust building Accent / phone presence



This is a high-trust phone role, so your spoken English must sound polished, natural, and easy for UK customers to trust.

Applicants with a clear, neutral, highly trusted English-speaking phone presence will stand out.



Tools



You’ll be using:

GoHighLevel

Slack

WhatsApp

Google Docs





This role is for someone who wants more than just a job.

If you are strong, coachable, hardworking, and want to grow with a company that values performance, this could be a great fit.



****** Please apply using the link below:







Only apply if you are confident on the phone, strong in English, and comfortable working UK hours in a results-focused sales environment.

title: Full-Time Content Creator (Nightlife, Events & Comedy) – Virtual Assistant
description:
Type: Full Time | Hours: 38 per week | Salary: $1,200 AUD/month | Paid: Weekly via Wise



?? READ THIS FIRST

If your portfolio is corporate explainers, fitness content, real estate walkthroughs, or talking-head videos — this is not the role for you.

We need someone who lives and breathes nightlife content, comedy-driven ads, and fast-turnaround event promotion. If that's not your lane, save us both the time.



About the Role

We are looking for a full-time Content Creator Virtual Assistant to own the entire content output for a single nightclub venue in Australia.

This is not a "cut clips and add subtitles" role. This is a full content creation role where you own the content engine for one venue.

You will be responsible for producing the complete content pipeline — paid ad creatives, organic social posts, short-form video (Reels, TikTok, Shorts), stories, event hype sequences, post-event recaps, UGC curation, and comedy/personality content that builds the venue's brand between events.

The venue runs on a weekly cycle. Every week has events. Every week needs a content arc — from teaser to hype to event night to recap. You need to understand that rhythm and own it without being told what to post every day.

This is a remote role based in the Philippines. You will not be on-site. Raw footage, photos, and assets will be provided to you by the venue team. However, you are expected to be proactive — you tell the venue what footage you need, what angles to capture, what 
nts to film. You direct the content pipeline. You don't just wait for files to land in your inbox.



This Role Is a Content Engine — Not Just Editing

This is not a general VA role.

This is not a strategy role.

This is not a "wait for briefs and execute" role.

The priority is:



Owning the weekly content cadence for the venue — you know what needs to go out and when

Building multi-day hype sequences before every event (teasers, DJ reveals, countdowns, last-call urgency posts)

Creating scroll-stopping paid ad content (video and static) designed to sell tickets

Producing high-energy organic social posts that drive engagement and build the venue's personality

Editing raw event footage into polished, fast-paced content — Reels, TikToks, Shorts, Stories

Creating post-event recaps, highlight reels, and UGC compilations that keep the venue top of mind

Producing comedy-driven and entertainment-style content that builds the brand between events

Proactively sending shot lists, content briefs, and footage requests to the venue team — you direct what gets captured on the ground

Sourcing and curating user-generated content from patrons (tagged stories, crowd footage, phone videos) and turning it into polished posts and ads



You must understand what makes people stop scrolling, laugh, share, and buy tickets.



Key Responsibilities

Primary Responsibilities (Core Focus)



Owning the weekly content calendar and storytelling arc for the venue

Sending content briefs, shot lists, and footage requests to the venue team before events — you tell them what you need captured

Editing short-form video content for Instagram Reels, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts from raw footage provided by the venue

Producing paid ad creatives (video and static) designed to convert and sell tickets

Creating organic social media posts that drive engagement

Building multi-day event hype sequences — teasers, DJ lineup reveals, countdown content, last-call urgency posts

Producing post-event recap content — highlight reels, UGC compilations, crowd reaction clips, best-of edits

Sourcing, curating, and repurposing user-generated content (patron videos, tagged stories, crowd footage)

Creating comedy, personality, and behind-the-scenes content that builds the venue's brand between events

Adding text overlays, subtitles, effects, transitions, and trending audio

Adapting one concept across multiple formats (Feed, Story, Square, Vertical, 9:16)

Delivering content on tight turnarounds — 6 to 24 hours when events demand it



Secondary Responsibilities (Support Tasks)



Organising content files in Google Drive

Tracking deliverables and deadlines in ClickUp

Following creative briefs and campaign deadlines from the marketing team

Preparing content exports correctly for each platform

Scheduling-ready content handoffs



(No paid ads management. No media buying. No marketing strategy.)



Required Experience (Non-Negotiable)

To be considered, you must have:



Experience creating content for nightclubs, DJs, festivals, live events, bars, comedy nights, or entertainment venues

Strong short-form video editing skills — not just trimming clips, but concept-to-finished-piece from raw footage

Experience producing paid ad creatives (video and/or static) that are designed to sell tickets or drive event attendance

Experience creating organic social content that drives real engagement

Understanding of weekly content cadence — you know how to tell a story across a week, building from teaser to hype to recap

Understanding of what makes comedy, entertainment, and nightlife content perform on social

Ability to work fast — this is an events business, not a "we'll get to it next week" environment

The ability to be proactive — you don't wait for someone to tell you what to make. You identify what's needed, request the right footage, and deliver

Evidence of content you've created that has gone viral or achieved significant reach (views, shares, engagement well above the norm for the account). Include specific examples with metrics if possible.

A portfolio that clearly demonstrates nightlife, events, comedy, or entertainment content



If your experience is mainly corporate, ecommerce product ads, fitness, real estate, or minimalist branding — this role is not suitable.



Nice to Have (Not Required)



Experience with ticket sale campaigns or event countdown sequences

Understanding of
/Instagram ad formats and specs

Experience creating UGC-style or native-feeling ad content

Motion graphics or animation skills

Experience working with Australian or Western nightlife brands

Basic graphic design ability (Canva or similar) for event posters, story graphics, and static ad creatives





Content Style We're Looking For



Fast-paced and high-energy

Bold text, punchy captions

Dark themes, neon, club lighting

Comedy timing and entertainment hooks

Native to the platform — not repurposed corporate content

Content that tells a story across the week, not random one-off posts

Content that feels like it belongs on a nightclub's page, not a brand agency's portfolio





What Success Looks Like in This Role



You own the content rhythm for the venue without being micromanaged

You proactively request the footage and assets you need from the venue team

You can turn raw footage into multiple pieces of polished content quickly

You understand the weekly cycle: tease ? hype ? event ? recap ? repeat

You consistently meet tight deadlines

You produce high-quality work without excessive revisions

You create content that gets people to stop scrolling and buy tickets

You've created content before that has achieved viral or outsized reach





Tools You'll Use



CapCut, Premiere Pro, Final Cut, or similar (video editing)

Adobe Photoshop / Illustrator or Canva (graphics)

Google Drive (file management and asset sharing)

ClickUp (task management)





Working Hours & Compensation



Full-time role (38 hours per week)

Must work during Australian business hours — AWST, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM (full overlap required)

Must be responsive and available during agreed working hours

Salary: $1,200 AUD per month, paid weekly via Wise

Long-term position for the right person, with room to grow



This role is not suitable for freelancers juggling multiple clients. We need someone committed, available, and fast.



How to Apply (Important – Read Carefully)

To apply, you must include:



A short introduction about yourself

Links to nightlife, event, comedy, or entertainment content you have created (not just edited)

At least one example of content you've created that went viral or achieved significant reach — include the metrics (views, shares, engagement)

At least one example of a paid ad creative you produced

At least one example of organic social content

An example of how you've proactively directed or briefed content capture (e.g. shot lists, content requests, footage briefs you've sent to a client or team)

The tools you used to create those examples

Your internet speed and equipment setup



Applications without relevant nightlife, event, or entertainment samples will not be considered.

To confirm you've read this job description, include the word "FREQUENCY" in the first line of your application.



Final Note

Do not send a generic portfolio and hope we find the relevant work. Curate your best nightlife, events, comedy, and entertainment pieces and put them front and centre.

If we have to dig through your portfolio to figure out whether you've done this kind of work before — you're already out.

title: Cold Calling Setter – MedSpa Industry (Base + Commission)
description:
We're a growing agency that helps medspas and aesthetic clinics across the US and Canada reactivate lost patients and grow their revenue. We're looking for a cold calling setter to join our team.



**The Role:



You'll be calling medspa owners and decision-makers to book qualified appointments for our closer. This is a setting position. Your job is to generate interest, handle initial objections, and get the prospect on a call with our team. You are not expected to close deals.

That said, if you perform well and want to grow, there is a clear path to move into a closing role.



**Compensation:



$250/month base salary plus $25 for every appointment that shows. The commission is where the real money is. A strong setter doing 20 to 30 showed appointments per month is earning $750 to $1,000/month total. There is no cap. Top performers also earn additional bonuses based on volume and consistency.



**Trial Period:



This role starts with a 30 day trial period where both sides can walk away if it's not the right fit. If you're producing results, this becomes a long term position with serious earning potential.



**Requirements:



Neutral, clear English with no accent. This is non-negotiable. You'll be speaking directly with business owners in the US and Canada, and you need to sound natural and confident on the phone.

Must be available to make calls during Eastern Time (ET) business hours.

Comfortable with cold calling and handling rejection.

Reliable internet and a quiet calling environment.

Experience in appointment setting, telemarketing, or outbound sales is a strong plus.



Preferred but not required: Female applicants are preferred given that our clients operate in the beauty and aesthetics space, but all qualified applicants are welcome to apply.



**What We Offer:



$250/month base plus $25 per showed appointment with no cap

Performance bonuses on top of commission

A path to a closer role for strong performers

A lean, no-BS team that rewards results

Fully remote. Work from anywhere as long as you can cover ET hours.



** To Apply:



Send a short voice recording (1 to 2 minutes) introducing yourself so we can hear how you sound on the phone. Tell us about any relevant experience and why you'd be a good fit.

title: Virtual Personal Assistant (Phillippines or Thailand) = Personal Admin + Bangkok Local Sourcing
description:
About the Role:

 American real estate investor and global traveler based in Bangkok is looking for a reliable, proactive Virtual Personal Assistant.



This is a fully remote, computer-based role focused on clearing everyday personal admin and “mental load” tasks so the principal can stay focused on high-value work. Most tasks are personal/lifestyle (not core business execution). You will work independently, use AI tools effectively, and occasionally source & coordinate local Bangkok service providers.

Hours: 20–30 hours per week (flexible, results-focused) Location: 100% remote (strong preference for Philippines or Thailand-based candidates for time-zone overlap with Bangkok UTC+7)

Key Responsibilities

• Handle recurring personal admin: cancel subscriptions/autopays, license/renewal tracking, bill follow-ups, research, online shopping/returns, and other items that tend to stay on the principal’s mind.

• Manage and update family + executive calendar; send concise proactive weekly summaries.

• Coordinate personal travel, bookings,
s, and general life admin.

• Source, screen, quote, and coordinate Bangkok-based service providers (handymen, plumbers, vendors, temporary help, etc.) for one-off household needs.

• Participate in short daily or 3x-weekly check-in calls (10–15 minutes).

• Collaborate with the principal using AI tools (primarily Grok and Claude) while applying strong human judgment.

Requirements

• Excellent written and spoken English (fluent).

• Proven experience as a personal or executive virtual assistant.

• Comfortable and effective using AI tools daily.

• Strong initiative, organization, and follow-through.

• Thai language or local Bangkok knowledge is a big plus (but not required).

• Reliable internet and quiet workspace.

What We Offer

• Virtual (Philippines/Remote): $7–$9 per hour (20–30 hrs/week) – paid weekly via Wise/Payoneer.

• Bangkok-based option: ?30,000–40,000 per month (~20-30 hrs/week, results-focused).

• Clear path to more hours, higher rate, and additional responsibilities as you prove reliability and initiative.

• Long-term collaboration with a straightforward, respectful principal.

How to Apply Only applications with a 30–60 second video will be reviewed. In English, please record and attach a short video covering:

1. Why you are a great fit for this role.

2. Your relevant experience.

3. How you believe you can best help.

Please start your application message with the word “READY” so I know you read the full post.

title: Operations Virtual Assistant (Part-Time) – Dropshipping / European Market
description:
We are looking for an experienced Operations Virtual Assistant with hands-on experience in dropshipping order management to join our growing eCommerce team.

This is not a customer support role. This position is fully focused on operational order management, supplier coordination, and internal task management.

The ideal candidate has direct experience working with Service Point (SP) as a supplier platform and understands the daily workflows of a dropshipping operation in the European market.



Responsibilities



Manage daily order operations via Service Point dashboard

Review and approve supplier quotations before the 7:00 AM CET deadline

Monitor and follow up on "Waiting for Tracking Update" orders

Communicate directly with suppliers via Slack to resolve delays and issues

Manage and update tasks in ClickUp — coordinate with the customer support agent and assign tasks with clear deadlines

Review and verify that all ClickUp tasks are completed within 24 hours

Monitor all order statuses (Not Quoted, On Hold, In Transit) across Service Point and Shopify

Update Shopify when products are out of stock or unavailable

Prepare weekly and monthly refund and order reports

Identify recurring issues and propose process improvements proactively





Requirements



Direct experience working with Service Point (SP) — this is mandatory

Minimum 2–3 years experience in dropshipping operations

Experience with Shopify order and product management

Experience using ClickUp for task management and team coordination

Strong written English communication skills

Highly organized with the ability to manage multiple order statuses simultaneously

Proactive mindset — able to identify problems and propose solutions independently

Ability to work part-time within European time zone (tasks must be completed before 7:00 AM CET)





What We Are Looking For

We are a growing eCommerce startup operating in the Swiss market. We need someone who takes full ownership of the operational side — not someone who waits for instructions.

You must be able to:



Work independently with minimal supervision

Spot issues before they escalate

Communicate proactively with suppliers and internal tea
mbers

Bring solutions, not just problems



This role is not for someone who needs constant guidance. We need a self-starter who manages the operation and keeps everything running smoothly on a daily basis.



Preferred Experience (Big Plus)



Experience with European dropshipping stores — especially Swiss or German market

Experience coordinating between supplier and customer support teams

Experience with order analytics and refund reporting

Familiarity with Slack for supplier communication





Work Setup



Part-time position

Remote / Virtual

Must be available to complete priority tasks before 7:00 AM CET daily





To Apply

Please include:



Your experience with Service Point — describe specifically how you used it

Your dropshipping operations experience

How you have used ClickUp or similar tools for task management

An example of a problem you identified and solved independently in a previous role

title: Creative Strategist and Production Coordinator (E-commerce)
description:
Creative Strategist & Production Coordinator (E-commerce)



We’re hiring a Creative Strategist & Production Coordinator to help us scale our paid creative output for Meta.



This role sits between creative strategy and execution. You will research winning ad concepts, identify ideas worth testing for our brand, adapt and write scripts, prepare voiceover and soundtrack drafts, create clear briefs for editors, and coordinate creator content production.



We’re looking for someone with strong creative taste, Meta ad understanding, good written English, and solid execution skills.



What You’ll Own



1. Creative Research & Strategy Support



You will research competitor and adjacent-brand ads, identify concepts and scripts that could work for our brand, and help build a clear pipeline of ideas worth testing. You should be able to recognize strong hooks, understand what makes an ad compelling, and think in terms of “same but different” rather than just copying what already exists.



2. Script Development



You will adapt winning concepts to our brand, write new scripts when needed, and create multiple hook variations for each concept. You should understand how to write for paid social, especially Meta, and know the difference between writing something that sounds good and writing something that is designed to convert.



3. Creative Assembly & Briefing



You will generate voiceovers, pair them with music, and assemble rough audio drafts that can be used for production. You will also turn concepts into clear, structured Notion briefs for editors, including line-by-line shot guidance, references, and relevant asset links. You should have enough editing sense to understand rhythm, pacing, and how a creative should come together.



4. Creator Coordination



You will help source creators for content batches following our SOPs, support outreach and follow-up, keep communication organized, and make sure deliverables are collected and stored properly. You should be comfortable coordinating moving parts, tracking progress, and helping maintain a reliable flow of fresh creator content.



Expected Deliverables :



The role is designed to support a consistent paid creative testing pipeline by following SOPs. The expected output includes:



- Consistent production of new video briefs (8 per month to start)

- Script development, voiceover generation, ongoing research

- 1 creator batch every 1–2 months, typically around 5 creators

- Creative Strategy research 



What We’re Looking For



You are likely a strong fit if you have experience working on Meta creatives for ecommerce brands and understand how paid social ads are structured to perform.



You should have:



- Experience with Meta /
 ad creatives

- strong understanding of hooks, angles, and direct-response creative

- good written English

- the ability to identify winning concepts and adapt them to a brand

- basic to strong editing skills

- Experience in following SOPs

- a good sense of rhythm, pacing, and creative flow

- strong organization and attention to detail

- the ability to turn ideas into clean, actionable briefs

- experience in DTC / ecommerce



Nice to Have



experience using DaVinci Resolve

experience with 11Labs or other voiceover tools

experience working with UGC creators / influencer coordination

experience building briefs in Notion



Payment



This role will start part-time (4 days a week, Monday to Thursday) moving toward full-time.

Compensation will depend on experience and proof of past results, but our initial target range is around $11.7/hour - $1500/month to start, with room to grow for the right person as results and responsibilities expand.



How to Apply



Please include:

a short note on your experience with Meta ad creatives

examples of ecommerce creative work you’ve been involved in

any relevant scriptwriting, creative strategy, editing, or briefing experience

tools you’ve used (for example: DaVinci, 11Labs, Notion, etc.) 



BrandJet I Propelling Brands into Space 

brandjet.eu

title: Operations & Administrative VA
description:
ROLE OVERVIEW



We are hiring an Operations & Administrative VA to support the backend of a growing coaching business and personal brand.



This role focuses on organization, systems, and execution, not content creation.



You will be responsible for helping maintain smooth day-to-day operations, managing digital tools, and supporting client and product workflows.



This role requires someone who is efficient, detail-oriented, and proactive with communication.

Upload and organize digital products



Support
 flows, lead capture, and basic automation setup

Website & Content Updates

Administrative Support

Communication & Problem Solving

REQUIRED SKILLS

Strong proficiency in Google Workspace (Docs, Drive, Sheets, Slides)



Strong organizational and administrative skills

PREFERRED (BUT NOT REQUIRED)

 platforms and basic lead nurturing

QUALITY & PERFORMANCE EXPECTATIONS (VERY IMPORTANT)

BILLABLE TIME POLICY

TO APPLY

What administrative tools and systems are you most comfortable using?

What is your weekly availability in EST?



Please include the word “ORGANIZED” at the top of your application so I know you read the full job description.

title: Video editor | META ADS
description:
Are you done with sidegigs and freelancing and are looking for a stable long-term position where you can grow in?



We are an Ecommerce business focused in the health and fitness niche. We're looking for a video editor that has proven experience editing direct response advertisements (video) and has worked for Ecommerce brands before. 



SALARY & BENEFITS



- Starting salary of 1000 USD every 4 weeks with max of 2000 USD based on your performance. 

- Unlimited performance bonuses 

- Fixed yearly salary increases & End-year company bonus 

- Growth potential, we are growing fast and want you to take part of that



REQUIREMENTS



You can create: Realistic AI UGC talking head, b-rolls, AI UGC video's, motion graphics, gigs 

You commit exclusively to our company: Not interested in that? Do not apply



You have the skills to: 



- Speak, write and communicate in clear and correct English

- Make multiple video's with limited footage (make something out of nothing) 

- Edit direct response video advertisements and have proven experience

title: Email Marketing Assistant **IMMEDIATE OPENING**
description:
Email Marketing Assistant



Date With the Muse



[
](
)



If you can commit to and live with the following principles, then you are the type of person who will be successful and help our company thrive. If you feel this level of engagement is not right for you or that you’re not willing or able to participate with us at this level, we are not a good fit for you.



Our expectation is that you will take the steps necessary to do what you say you are going to do and be accountable for your actions. You are committed to excellence and take pride in a job well done.



We understand that not every person is ready for this level of performance and we appreciate the honesty of those who d
 this is not the right place for them. On the other hand, you would make an ideal candidate to join our company if you are willing to commit to the following principles:



- Accountability: see it, own it, solve it, do it

- Become part of the solution

- Respect for others and their feelings

- Act now.

- Ask the question: “What else can I do?”

- Ask the questions: “What coaching do you have for me?” and “What can I do better?”

- Personal ownership and pride

- Reject average

- Show others that you care



Part-Time | Remote

We are seeking a highly detail-oriented, systems-driven
 Marketing Specialist who is deeply experienced in Mailchimp and can own the execution, tracking, and optimization of our
 campaigns from start to finish.

This is not a beginner role. You must already know how to use Mailchimp at an advanced level and be able to execute without hand-holding.



Role Overview

You will be responsible for building, scheduling, tagging, automating, and tracking
 campaigns inside Mailchimp to support launches, lead nurturing, and ongoing marketing initiatives.

Success in this role means:

Campaigns go out accurately and on time

Audience segmentation is intentional and strategic

Automations run cleanly and reliably

Data is tracked and reported in a way that informs decisions



Core Responsibilities

Campaign Execution

Build and schedule
 campaigns in Mailchimp

Format
s for clarity, readability, and brand consistency

Ensure all links, tags, and tracking are correctly implemented

Conduct final QA before sending (no broken links, formatting errors, or tag issues)

Audience Segmentation & Tagging

Apply and manage tags based on lead source, behavior, and engagement

Maintain clean audience organization (no duplication, no tag chaos)

Segment lists for targeted campaigns (launches, webinars, nurture sequences)

Automations

Build and maintain automated
 sequences (welcome flows, nurture sequences, event follow-ups)

Ensure automations trigger correctly based on tags and user behavior

Troubleshoot and fix any automation issues proactively

Data Tracking & Reporting

Track key metrics: open rates, click-through rates, conversions, unsubscribe rates

Provide clear weekly reports with insights (not just numbers)

Identify what’s working, what’s not, and where to optimize

Systems & Maintenance

Maintain a clean, organized Mailchimp backend

Document processes and ensure repeatable workflows

Flag issues before they become problems



Required Experience

Advanced, hands-on experience with Mailchimp (non-negotiable)

Proven ability to:

Build campaigns from scratch

Set up and manage automations

Use tags and segmentation strategically

Track and interpret campaign performance data

Strong attention to detail (you catch what others miss)

Ability to follow SOPs while also thinking critically when something doesn’t make sense



Ideal Candidate

Systems thinker (you see how all the pieces connect)

Proactive (you don’t wait to be told something is broken)

Organized and methodical

Comfortable working independently and owning outcomes

Able to execute quickly without sacrificing precision



Definition of Done

A task is complete when:

The campaign is sent or automation is live

All tags and segmentation are correctly applied

All links and tracking are verified

Performance is tracked and reported



Commitment & Reliability (Non-Negotiable)

This role requires absolute reliability and follow-through.

Campaigns will often be scheduled days or weeks in advance

You are expected to complete work ahead of deadlines—not at the last minute

You must ensure you have working access to Mailchimp at all times (login issues are not an acceptable blocker)

If something is not scheduled when it should be, that is a failure of the role

If an issue arises, you are expected to flag it early—not when a campaign is about to go out

This is not a “log in when you feel like it” position.

 This is a time-sensitive execution role tied directly to revenue.

## To Apply



Qualified applicants are encouraged to apply as soon as possible. To express interest please submit 



a cover letter addressed to the CEO detailing why you are uniquely qualified for this role,



a resume,



and a DISC profile 



 materials to 
 with the headline



Interviews will be conducted on a rolling basis. This position begins ASAP for the right candidate. 



Applications received through this platform/website will not move forward.

title: CAD/Renderer Specialist
description:
APPLY HERE: 
/apply/



Blueprint Workforce is a U.S.-based workforce partner for construction, design, and trade businesses. We don't place generic virtual assistants. We place specialists into defined roles with structured onboarding, clear SOPs, and ongoing support.

We're hiring a CAD/Renderer Specialist to produce construction drawings, 3D models, and photorealistic renderings for U.S.-based architecture, design, and construction firms.



This is a technical production role. You'll draft, model, render, and revise. You'll turn sketches and redlines into buildable drawings and client-ready visuals. Precision and speed both matter.

WHAT YOU'LL DO:



-Draft and produce construction documents, floor plans, elevations, sections, and detail drawings

-Create 3D models from architectural plans, sketches, or reference images

-Produce photorealistic renderings for client presentations, marketing, and design approvals

-Revise drawings based on redlines, markups, and design changes

-Maintain drawing standards, layer management, and file organization per firm protocols

-Coordinate with architects, designers, and project managers on drawing sets and deadlines

-Prepare drawing packages for permitting, bidding, and construction

-Perform quality checks on drawings for accuracy, dimensions, and code compliance



TOOLS YOU SHOULD KNOW (some or all):



-AutoCAD

-Revit

-SketchUp

-Rhino

-V-Ray, Lumion, or Enscape (rendering)

-Bluebeam Revu



WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR:



-2+ years of experience in CAD drafting, 3D modeling, or architectural rendering

-Strong proficiency in at least two of the tools listed above

-Portfolio or work samples demonstrating construction drawings and/or renderings (required)

-Understanding of construction documentation standards and building systems

-Architecture, engineering, or interior design educational background preferred

You deliver clean, accurate work and manage revisions without losing detail

You can manage multiple drawing sets and deadlines simultaneously

-Strong written English communication for coordinating with U.S.-based teams

-You're comfortable working U.S. business hours (aligned with your client's time zone)

-Stable internet connection, professional remote work setup, and a machine capable of running CAD/rendering software



WHAT BLUEPRINT PROVIDES:



-Full-time, long-term placement (this is not project-based or seasonal)

-Structured 48-hour onboarding with role-specific training and SOPs

-Dedicated support specialist for your first 90 days

-Regular check-ins: Day 3, Day 30, Day 60, Day 90, then quarterly

-Payroll processed through Gusto (direct deposit)

-10 days PTO annually (after 90-day probation) + 5 sick days (available immediately)

-A company that invests in your success, not just your output



WHY BLUEPRINT IS DIFFERENT:



Most offshore staffing companies hand you a job title and disappear. Blueprint doesn't.

Every placement goes through an 81-question behavioral assessment matched across 6 dimensions. You're not just "available"; you're matched to a role where you'll actually perform and grow.



You get structured onboarding, real support, and a team that treats you as a professional, not a commodity.

We retain 98% of our placements at 6 months. That's not an a
. It's because we build systems that set you up to succeed.



HOW TO APPLY:



APPLY HERE: 
/apply/



Complete your application through our platform. The process includes a behavioral assessment that matches you to the right role and the right client. No generic applications. No guessing.



If you're ready to be placed, not just hired, apply now.


/apply/

title: SHOPIFY OPERATIONS MANAGER
description:
WHO WE ARE

Modern Viking is a fast-growing premium ecommerce brand selling Norse-inspired axes, knives, and gear. We're not just a men's brand — we're one of the go-to destinations for gift giving. Birthdays, Father's Day, Christmas, anniversaries — people come to us when they want to give something bold, meaningful, and unforgettable. Our customers are gift buyers, collectors, and people who live life on their own terms.

We're a lean, ambitious team and we're scaling fast. We need someone who can run our Shopify store like they own it — not someone who waits to be told what to do.



THE ROLE

This is not a basic VA position. You will own the day-to-day operations of our Shopify store, help grow our conversion rate and average order value, manage customer service, coordinate freelancers, and keep the business running smoothly so the founder can focus on growth.

If you're results-driven, proactive, and take pride in making a store perform — this role is for you.



WHAT YOU'LL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR

Store Operations



Product listings, collections, pricing, and inventory management

App installation, setup, and ongoing optimisation

Upsell and cross-sell setup (post-purchase flows, cart upsells, bundles)

Conversion rate optimisation — product pages, mobile experience, trust signals

Promotions, discount codes, and seasonal campaign setup

Weekly performance reporting — revenue, conversion rate, AOV



Customer Service



Managing all customer
s and chat tickets

Handling refunds, damaged orders, and disputes

Responding to reviews

Building and maintaining our CS response library



Team & Admin



Briefing and managing freelancers (designers, copywriters)

Keeping projects on track and deadlines met

Documenting processes and building SOPs

Light design work in Canva — banners, product images, promotional assets





YOU'RE RIGHT FOR THIS ROLE IF



You have real hands-on Shopify experience and can name the apps you've used

You think in revenue — you ask "will this make money?" before you start a task

You write customer responses that sound human, not robotic

You send updates before being asked

You can manage freelancers without needing to be micromanaged yourself

You've actually improved a store metric and can show it





DO NOT APPLY IF



You've never worked inside a Shopify store backend

You need constant direction to know what to work on

You treat deadlines as suggestions

You copy-paste generic customer service templates

You're looking for a quiet, low-effort side gig





WHAT WE OFFER



Starting pay: $700–$1,000/month for 20 hours per week

100% remote, flexible working hours

Direct access to the founder — no corporate layers

Real responsibility from day one

Clear path to full-time as the business grows

You'll be a core part of a scaling brand, not just a task monkey





HOW TO APPLY



The name of a Shopify store you've worked on and one specific metric you improved (conversion rate, AOV, CS response time — anything measurable)

The upsell or post-purchase apps you've used and why

A Loom video (3–5 minutes) where you walk us through a Shopify store you've managed, show us something you improved, and tell us why you'd be the right person for Modern Viking. No Loom video = no response.



We are looking for someone who moves fast, owns their work, and genuinely cares about results. If that's you, we want to hear from you.

title: Pre-Press Specialist
description:
Overview

We’re looking for a detail-oriented Pre-Press Specialist to support our production team by preparing high-quality, print-ready files for multiple printers. This role is critical in ensuring accuracy, speed, and consistency across all artwork before it goes into production.



Key Responsibilities

Prepare and convert design files (from Adobe Illustrator and Photoshop) into print-ready formats based on specific printer requirements

Separate artwork into proper layers (base white, design, and clear top layers) according to production specs

Create realistic mock-ups to showcase how designs will appear on cups for customer approval

Process files from multiple sources, including:

Custom designer submissions

Website orders (customer-created designs)

Wholesale artwork requests via

Ensure all files meet quality and technical standards before production

Communicate with the team in real time to resolve file issues and maintain workflow efficiency



Performance Expectations

Handle 150–180 files per week per specialist

Maintain an average turnaround time of ~10 minutes per file (depending on complexity)

Support continuous production needs, helping cover gaps in operational hours



Requirements

Strong proficiency in Adobe Photoshop and Adobe Illustrator

Solid understanding of print production and different printer specifications/formats

High attention to detail with strong quality control skills

Ability to work efficiently under time pressure and manage high-volume tasks

Flexible availability, including potential weekend work and coverage across time zones

title: Full-Time Virtual Assistant: Admin, Operations & AI-Assisted Work
description:
About the Role

I'm looking for a sharp, highly adaptable Virtual Assistant to take ownership of the day-to-day work that keeps my business running. Full-time, starting at $450/month, with real room to grow as you prove yourself.

The core trait I'm hiring for is adaptability. I need someone who can do basically anything I throw at them, figure out what they don't already know, and get it done. If you need detailed instructions for every task, this role isn't for you. I won't always be available to answer questions, so you need to go from problem to solution on your own.

What You'll Be Doing

A rotating mix of admin, operations, and content coordination. Examples:



Monitoring and managing my inbox: triaging, flagging what needs me, handling what doesn't

Drafting and sending
 replies on my behalf

Coordinating with my team and instructing them on tasks I assign

Tracking progress across projects and keeping me updated throughout the day

General administrative work

Placing orders and managing purchases

Posting content to my blog. I run a blog, and you'll be transcribing posts from my
s or voice notes and publishing them. You won't be writing the content, just formatting and posting.

Working in Google Sheets and Excel for tracking, reporting, and data entry

Using AI tools (especially Claude and Claude Code) to get through tasks faster. This is important. A lot of what I do is AI-assisted, and you need to be comfortable working that way.

Anything else that comes up. This role is deliberately broad.



What I'm Looking For



Excellent written and spoken English

Strong administrative and organizational skills

Good with computers in general

Confident with Google Sheets and Excel

Strong with AI tools, especially Claude and Claude Code. If you haven't used Claude Code specifically, you need to be the type of person who will learn it quickly and properly.

College degree preferred (strong experience can override this)

Extremely resourceful. You can Google, investigate, and work things out on your own.

Adaptable. The work varies a lot day to day.

Reliable, detail-oriented, and discreet with sensitive information

Comfortable receiving instructions through voice notes



What I'm NOT Looking For



Someone who needs constant check-ins

Someone who asks three clarifying questions before attempting anything

Someone who waits for perfect instructions before starting



Working Hours

Roughly 8 hours a day, sometimes split across the day. A typical day looks like:



2 hours in the morning

1 hour around lunch

2 hours in the afternoon

I'll check in with my employees morning and evening UK daytime. No late evening work required. You just need to be responsive during the day and stay coordinated with me and the team.

Long-term role for the right person

Send a message that includes:

Your working hours and timezone

title: 2D/3D Animator (Blender + After Effects)
description:
About Us

We are looking for a full-time Animator for our YouTube channels:



• 

• 



We make documentary-style videos that explain complex stories in a clear and engaging way.





Role Overview

This role focuses on two core tasks:

• Geolayer3 map animations (2D / 2.5D / 3D)

• 2D & 3D animations in After Effects using custom models, icons and elements you create or modify.



You will read a brief, visualize the sequence, and deliver polished shots that match our style. You must be able to handle 3d modes, animation, and compositing as needed.





Key Responsibilities

• Build cinematic map animations: routes, movements, markers, labels, zooms, elevation, highlights

• Create or modify 3D models of vehicles, equipment, terrain, and props (modeling, kitbashing, UVs, textures, basic rigging)

• Animate 3D shots: camera moves, turntables, reveals, exploded views

• Composite icons, labels, and images for clarity

• Keep project files clean and organized (naming, versioning, tidy scenes)

• Work closely with editors and producers; share WIPs and take notes

• Track tasks in Notion, deliver files to Dropbox

• Optimize scenes for fast iteration and re-renders.





Minimum Requirements

• 5+ years in 3D and motion graphics for documentaries/explainers

• Expert in After Effects (compositing, 2.5D camera, motion blur, overlays)

• Expert in 3D (modeling, UVs, texturing, shading, lighting, rendering, basic rigging)

• Strong skills in Illustrator/Photoshop (icons, labels, map elements)

• Strong visual storytelling and ability to execute independently

• Fluent English (written and spoken)

• Daily working overlap with Cyprus business hours (Mon–Fri)

• Familiarity with ChatGPT and AI tools for workflow support





Nice to Have

• Experience with stylized or data-based mapping workflows

• Interest in defense, aviation, military history, or geospatial visualization





Your Setup

• Workstation with 64 GB RAM minimum (more preferred), strong GPU/CPU

• Fast, stable internet (30-50 Mbps upload recommended)





Contract & Hours

• Full-time, remote

• Core overlap with Cyprus time required

• Must follow YTMG’s confidentiality, IP, communication, and schedule policies





How to Apply

Please send your CV and showreel/portfolio, along with:

1. Two end-to-end examples: one map animation and one 3D model-based animation (brief ? what you built ? tools used)

2. A short breakdown of a complex shot (goal, approach, modeling steps, pipeline)

3. Your hardware specs and a recent internet speed test screenshot

4. One sentence confirming you have read and accept the expectations listed above

title: Senior Appraisal Coordinator
description:
**ONLY APPLY VIA THE LINK PROVIDED BELOW. DM'S SENT ON ONLINE JOBS WILL NOT BE RESPONDED TO**



If you’ve worked inside a US Appraisal Management Company and you know the difference between a 1004 and a Field Review, a TAT and a due date extension, an ENB file and a standard PDF delivery — this role was written for you.

We’re not looking for someone we need to teach the industry. We’re looking for someone who already speaks it fluently and is ready to own it.



Senior Appraisal Coordinator



Company: Pahroo Appraisal and Consultancy, LLC - Chicago USA.

Location: Remote (US hours)

Type: Full-Time

Hours: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM CST, Monday to Friday

Salary: PHP 55,000 – PHP 95,000



ABOUT PAHROO

PahRoo Appraisal & Consultancy LLC is a Chicago-based real estate appraisal firm operating across residential and commercial markets in Illinois and Texas. We work with AMCs, direct lenders, and private clients, and we are growing — deliberately and with the right people.



We run a ROWE culture: Results Only Work Environment. We don’t count hours. We count outcomes. If you’re a professional who delivers without being micromanaged, you’ll thrive here.



THE ROLE

This is a senior front office position with real ownership. You will be the operational engine of our Appraisal Operations Department — the person who keeps orders moving, clients updated, appraisers scheduled, and nothing falling through the cracks.



You’ll work directly with our Appraisal Operations Manager, hold independent decision-making authority on day-to-day coordination, and play a hands-on role in shaping how our front office runs as we scale.



This is not a task-ticker role. It is a thinking role for someone who takes pride in running a tight operation.



WHAT YOU’LL OWN

– Full order lifecycle management across ANOW — from bid acceptance through report delivery and closure

– Independent bid decisions on standard residential assignments; zero waiting on someone else to move your queue

– Inspection scheduling and appraiser route optimization for our Chicago and Dallas markets

– Outreach calling and pipeline management across our AMC and direct client accounts

– Report delivery via AMC portals in all required formats — PDF, XML, ENB

– Revision management end-to-end — you communicate, you track, you close

– Zoho CRM ownership for outreach tracking and lead pipeline management

– Keeping ANOW audit-ready at all times — data integrity is non-negotiable here



WHAT WE NEED FROM YOU

– Minimum 2 years of experience in a US AMC, appraisal office, or appraisal management environment — this is a hard requirement, not a preference

– Working knowledge of US residential appraisal order types: 1004/URAR, FHA, Field Review, New Construction, Desktop

– Hands-on experience with ANOW or a comparable appraisal CRM (Mercury Network, Appraisal Inbox, etc.)

– Professional written and spoken English — you are client-facing from day one

– The ability to manage 20+ active orders simultaneously without losing your composure or your accuracy

– Availability to work full CST business hours: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Monday to Friday

– CRM experience in Zoho, Salesforce, or equivalent is a strong advantage



WHAT YOU GET

– PHP 55,000–95,000/month commensurate with experience — a senior rate for a senior hire

– Full-time, long-term contractor engagement — this is a career position, not a project

– Direct access to a senior leadership team actively investing in the right systems and the right people

– Deep, specialized expertise in the US appraisal market — one of the most defensible and transferable skill sets in offshore real estate operations

– A ROWE culture — your results define your day, not a timesheet



START DATE

Immediate. We are moving quickly on this hire and interviewing on a rolling basis. If you are the right person, we will not wait.



APPLY NOW:


/Pahroo/preview-job?jid=FKUpnx7g4/NCLEepnH23hxplCmXbkVeC5DbwzT5C4DesIFQl

title: MedTech Account Manager
description:
YouMed Ltd

We are an established, privately owned company specialising in working with worldwide med-tech and diagnostics businesses to support their entry into the UK market. Typical clients are smaller start-ups who rely on our guidance and expertise to expand into the UK market. Offering tailored strategic consultancy, product launch support, bespoke sales and distribution programmes, our success is built on having a strong understanding of the products, the UK clinical marketplace and a network of established relationships.



As we continue our growth we are seeking a full-time MedTech Account Manager to work closely with our Business Development team and support the Business Director in building relationships with potential clients and develop an ongoing business pipeline. 



Job Specification

The role is based on working 40 hours per week, working 6.00 am – 2.00 pm UK GMT. 

Typical activities will include:

• Developing identified warm leads into evaluation sites / trial sites and sales

o Presenting our client’s assays to Clinical Directors, Clinicians and Lab Teams within the NHS and Private MedTech sector. 

o Preparation and delivery of company presentations. 

o Understanding the client’s product and their requirements, identifying opportunities for areas of support.

o Maintaining regular client communication to find out the status of their projects.

o Leading evaluations and trials at sites including training / distribution of products.



• Keeping clear and up to date records, liaising with colleagues as appropriate.



The Person

To be successful in the role we are looking for someone who can demonstrate the following essential qualities and skills: 

• Senior MedTech Sales experience - An understanding of the MedTech/Diagnostics sector.  Proven competency in Business Development / Medical Sales or similar.

• Experience with presenting and closing sales. 

• Self-starter who is comfortable working independently on a remote basis.

• Strong communicator, able to network and develop good working relationships.

• Excellent presentation skills, spoken and written English.

• Takes an organised, systematic approach keeping thorough records. 



In addition, the following would be desirable:

• Ability to demonstrate an understanding of the UK healthcare market.

• Ability to demonstrate an understanding of the development and launch cycle of a MedTech product to be able to identify opportunities for support.



Qualifications required

• Degree educated (Medical or associated)

• Experience of working in MedTech / Diagnostics.  Preference would be having worked for or having experience with any of   the following companies however any experience in commercial Med Tech is sufficient (surgical etc):-

Roche Diagnostics

Abbott Laboratories

Siemens Healthineers

Danaher Corporation

Thermo Fisher Scientific

Becton, Dickinson and Company

bioMérieux

Sysmex Corporation

Qiagen

Hologic



• Advanced English – both written and spoken

• Competency in Microsoft Office packages



SALARY -  To be determined / negotiated depending on experience.

title: Virtual Stylist & Wardrobe Curator (Hourly, Temporary)
description:
About the Role

I’m looking for a thoughtful, fashion-minded virtual stylist to help me bring order, creativity, and fresh vision to my existing wardrobe. I’ve already photographed my clothes, shoes, bags, and jewelry. Now, I need your eye to help me put it all together.



This is a remote position where you’ll help me create styled looks for different parts of my life: speaking engagements, church services, real estate client meetings, casual outings, travel days, and formal events. We’ll use the Indyx iOS app to build and organize my wardrobe, and I’ll cover the premium subscription if needed.



You won’t need to shop for me or build a wardrobe from scratch. I simply need help refining and coordinating what I already own into outfits that are appropriate, elegant, and true to who I am.



A Little About Me



I’m a minister, real estate broker, and author based in Tennessee. I show up in many different spaces — from pulpits to closing tables to Sunday brunch — and I want my wardrobe to reflect confidence, modesty, purpose, and grace. My style is a blend of sophistication and professionalism, with a touch of Afrocentric flair.



What You’ll Be Doing:

Access my wardrobe photos inside the Indyx app and help me organize them by category, color, and occasion

Create polished and appropriate outfits for specific events like:



Sunday services

Real estate closings and networking

Speaking engagements (in person and virtual)

Brunch or lunch with friends

Travel and formal events

Build lookbooks and mood boards in the app so I can easily find what to wear

Tag each item and outfit with helpful details like occasion, season, and location

Suggest styling adjustments, accessory pairings, or layering options

Use the app’s built-in photo editor to clean up background images and help the catalog look its best

Provide brief notes or explanations for each outfit so I understand how to pull it together myself



What I’m Looking For:



Someone with experience in styling, wardrobe curation, or fashion coordination

A person who understands how to work with modest, professional, ministry-appropriate clothing

Comfortable using iOS and willing to work inside the Indyx app (training provided if needed)

Organized and good at communicating clearly and kindly

Someone who enjoys helping others look and feel their best

Bonus if you have experience building digital lookbooks or working with speakers or professionals

Details:



This is a short-term project paid by the hour

Please include your hourly rate when you apply



We’ll begin with a paid trial task (such as styling 3 to 5 looks) and move from there



I’d love to get started right away, with the project expected to last around 3 to 4 weeks, depending on how we pace it



To Apply



Please send:



A short introduction about yourself and your styling background

Your hourly rate

Any examples of work you’ve done; especially for clients who speak, teach, or lead professionally

Confirmation that you use an iOS device and are open to working inside the Indyx app

title: Video Editor (Premiere Pro + After Effects | Captions + Motion | Full-Time Remote)
description:
'm looking for a dedicated, detail-oriented video editor to join De Videofabriek — a Dutch video production company — as a long-term, full-time team member.



This is not just an editing role. You'll be working closely with me on a wide range of video content: client deliverables, Instagram/LinkedIn content, and multi-format exports. You'll be involved in the process, not just handed files.



**What you'll do:**



- Edit videos based on a structured proxy workflow

- Add captions (accurate timing, clean styling) and motion graphics/animations

- Export multiple formats per project efficiently

- When no edit jobs are queued: help build and optimize templates, workflows, and systems in Premiere Pro, After Effects, and Notion

- Communicate clearly despite a language difference — most content is in Dutch and English; scripts and SRTs are always provided, and I'll brief you thoroughly on context



**What I'm looking for:**



- Strong Premiere Pro skills — fast, clean, organized

- Basic After Effects knowledge is preferred

- Experience with captions (not just auto-generated — properly timed, styled and animated)

- Comfortable working in Notion for task and project management

- Proactive, reliable, and communicative — someone who thinks along, not just executes

- Available during most of the overlapping hours, proactive and independent in other hours

- Can handle Dutch captions and subtitles without understanding the language (scripts + briefings always provided in English)



**Nice to have:**



- Experience with proxy workflows

- Familiarity with Instagram and LinkedIn-style short-form video. YouTube is a plus

- Understanding of Dutch (not required, but a big plus)



**What we offer:**



- Full-time remote position (~40 hrs/week)

- €750/month (long-term, stable work)

- Close collaboration — you won't be left in the dark

- Room to grow as systems and workload scale



**How to apply:**



Send a short message explaining your editing background + 2-3 examples of work that show captions and/or motion graphics. Bonus: tell me one thing you'd check to improve in a typical editing workflow.

title: Real Estate Business Development Specialist
description:
We are a U.S.-based real estate investment company looking for a high-performing Business Development Specialist to join our team.



This is NOT a typical VA role.



This is a sales + lead generation position working directly with off-market property opportunities.



What You’ll Be Doing:



- Outbound calling using our CRM & dialer

- Speaking with property owners in niche situations:

- Pre-foreclosures

- Tax delinquent properties

- Probates / inherited homes

- Vacant & distressed properties

- Qualifying leads (condition, timeline, motivation, price)

- Taking detailed notes and updating CRM

- Researching hard-to-reach property owners (skip tracing, online investigation)

- Setting appointments / passing qualified leads to acquisitions team



What We’re Looking For



- Fluent English (clear, confident speaking — minimal accent)

- 1+ years experience in real estate cold calling, acquisitions, or similar sales role

- Strong objection handling & communication skills

- Comfortable making high-volume outbound calls daily

- Experience with dialers/CRMs (CallTools, Mojo, BatchDialer, REISift, etc. is a PLUS)

- Strong research & problem-solving ability (finding hard-to-reach owners)

- Reliable, consistent, and results-driven mindset



Requirements:



- Fast & stable internet connection

- Quiet work environment

- Dual monitors (required)

- Headset for professional call quality

- Ability to work U.S. hours (CST preferred)



Compensation:



- Base pay: $5-6/ hour

- Commission per qualified lead / % / deal

- Performance bonuses for top producers

- Opportunity for long-term growth within the company



What Makes This Role Different:



- You’ll be working directly with a high-level real estate system (data-driven, AI-supported)

- Consistent lead flow — no guessing who to call

Clear KPIs and growth path

- We reward performance — top reps earn significantly more with commissions



Who This Is Perfect For:



- Experienced real estate cold callers who want more opportunity + upside

- Sales reps who are competitive and want to grow

- People who take ownership and don’t need micromanagement



Do NOT Apply If:



- You have no cold calling or sales experience

- You are not comfortable talking to distressed property owners

- You are inconsistent or unreliable



We are hiring immediately and looking for long-term tea
mbers only.



If you’re serious about growth and making money, apply now.

title: CRM / Database Specialist
description:

Summary – We are looking for a CRM / Database Specialist who can design, organize, and maintain a clean, scalable CRM system. This role requires someone experienced in database architecture, data hygiene, and workflow automation focused on backend data integrity (not marketing campaigns).



You will be responsible for structuring the CRM, standardizing data, maintaining clean records, and ensuring the system supports long-term growth and reporting.



The client is currently using ActiveCampaign and may migrate to GoHighLevel, so experience with both platforms is highly preferred.



Please read all REQUIRED conditions below before applying 



REQUIRED: Proven experience managing CRM systems and database structures

REQUIRED: Experience with data cleanup, deduplication, and merging records

REQUIRED: Strong understanding of CRM architecture (fields, tags, custom objects, relationships)

REQUIRED: Experience building workflows/automations for database updates and hygiene (not
 marketing)

REQUIRED: Experience with ActiveCampaign and/or GoHighLevel

REQUIRED: Comfortable using a time-tracking system

REQUIRED: Ability to work 10 hours per week consistently



1. When you message us to apply, you MUST include the sentence below in the subject line or first sentence of your message. Applications that do not include this will be automatically discarded:



“I can work 10 hours per week, I’m comfortable using a time-tracking system, and I can attend the stand-up call. I am reliable, proactive, and detail-oriented. My experience includes CRM systems (list platforms such as ActiveCampaign, GoHighLevel, etc.), and I have experience with database management including deduplication, standardization, workflows, and CRM structuring.”



2. A link to your CV, resume, or portfolio showing relevant CRM/database work (required) 



Responsibilities:

• Design and maintain CRM database structure (fields, tags, pipelines, relationships)

• Standardize data setup, naming conventions, and system organization

• Perform ongoing database cleanup including deduplication and record merging

• Build and maintain account/contact hierarchies

• Create workflows and automations for database hygiene (e.g., tagging, field updates, lifecycle stages)

• Audit CRM data regularly to ensure accuracy and consistency

• Support CRM migration (if applicable) from ActiveCampaign to GoHighLevel

• Document processes and maintain an organized system structure



Preferred Experience & Skills:

• Experience with BOTH ActiveCampaign and GoHighLevel

• Experience handling CRM migrations and data mapping

• Familiarity with Zapier, Make, APIs, or other integration tools

• Understanding of reporting structures and segmentation strategies

• High attention to detail and strong problem-solving skills



Stretch Goals/responsibilities

• Build scalable systems to support marketing and sales teams

• Assist with integrations between CRM and other tools

• Conduct periodic CRM audits and optimization reviews

title: WordPress Web Designer (Conversion-Focused + Basic SEO & Tracking Setup)
description:
WordPress Web Designer (Conversion-Focused + Basic SEO & Tracking Setup)

About Us

Our current website needs improving — not a full rebuild, but a clear, simple, conversion-focused upgrade.



The Project

Improve our website design and structure

Make it clear, modern, and conversion-focused

Set up tracking properly (Google Analytics & Tag Manager)

Apply basic SEO best practices

1. Website Design & Structure

Improve homepage layout and clarity

Create a clean, modern, professional design

Ensure mobile responsiveness

Improve user journey with clear call-to-actions

Add strong CTAs throughout

3. Tracking Setup

Set up Google Tag Manager

Set up Google Analytics (GA4)

Track key actions:

Page views

Button clicks

Form submissions or bookings

4. Basic SEO Setup (Not advanced SEO)

Ensure proper heading structure (H1, H2, etc.)

Improve page speed basics

Make site SEO-friendly for future growth

5. Light Copy Support

Help improve wording and structure of pages

Ensure clear, professional English

Work with our input (we will provide direction and ideas)

Requirements

Must have:

Strong experience with WordPress

Experience with Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics (GA4)

Understanding of conversion-focused design

Good written English

Nice to have:

Basic SEO knowledge

Experience improving landing pages

Copywriting ability (clear and simple writing)

Budget and Timeline

Fixed price: $300

Timeline: 2–4 weeks

Potential for ongoing work after project completion

What success looks like

Website looks modern and professional

Clear message and user journey

Visitors understand the offer quickly

Tracking is set up correctly

Site is ready for future SEO growth

Important



This is not a full advanced SEO project.



We are looking for:



Clean design

Clear structure

Proper tracking setup

How to apply



Please include:



2–3 WordPress websites you have worked on

Your experience with Google Tag Manager and GA4

A short explanation of how you improve a website for conversions

The word “Workshop” at the top of your application
