title: Implementation Specialist
description:
APPLICATION LINK: 

NOTE: applicants who do not complete the application form above will not be considered.



ROLE OVERVIEW

The Implementation Specialist owns the post-sale onboarding and rollout of Coast, from kickoff through go-live and handoff to Customer Success. This is a technical, project-oriented role—not frontline support. You'll spend the majority of your time translating complex, real-world operational workflows from industries like facilities management, manufacturing, healthcare field services, and hospitality into Coast configurations that actually work for teams in the field.



That means running discovery to understand how a maintenance team or field operation really works, then designing and building automations, configuring integrations, and structuring the platform so customers can adopt it quickly and confidently. You'll juggle multiple implementations in parallel, ranging from fast-moving rollouts to complex, multi-phase deployments—and you'll be expected to manage both without dropping the ball.



The Implementation Specialist will work closely with Customer Success, Product, and Engineering, and will primarily support customers in North America while being based in the Philippines.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES 

- Lead end?to?end implementation projects from kickoff through go?live and handoff, managing scope, milestones, and risks across multiple simultaneous accounts

- Run deep discovery sessions to understand customer workflows, operational environments, and technical constraints—then translate that into a concrete configuration and automation plan.

- Design and build automations using Coast's native triggers and rules engine.

- Configure Coast fully: settings, user roles and permissions, data structures, workflow templates, and integrations aligned to each customer's operational reality.

- Coordinate data migration, testing, and validation with customer and internal technical teams.

- Learn unfamiliar industries quickly. Our customers work in facilities, manufacturing plants, healthcare field operations, and hospitality. You don't need to know all of them on day one, but you need to be able to get up to speed fast, ask the right questions, and map their workflows accurately into the platform.

- Facilitate onboarding and training sessions for admins and end users, tailoring your approach for both technical and non-technical audiences.

- Track implementation progress, communicate status and next steps clearly, and proactively adjust plans when priorities or constraints change.

- Identify and troubleshoot rollout issues, coordinating with Support, Product, and Engineering as needed.

Capture repeatable patterns, edge cases, and feedback to improve implementation playbooks, templates, and product roadmap inputs.

- Use tools like Coast, Google Workspace, Zoo
et, Avoma, Intercom, and Zendesk every day to manage tasks, communicate with customers, and keep internal stakeholders aligned.

- Work a schedule with meaningful overlap with US business hours so you can join customer calls, internal standups, and cross?functional working sessions.



REQUIREMENTS 

Must-haves:

- 3+ years in SaaS implementation, onboarding, professional services, or a customer-facing technical role

- Proven hands-on experience configuring complex no-code/low-code platforms such as Airtable, 
, Notion, ClickUp, or similar—not just administering them, but designing workflows and structures for real operational use cases.

- Demonstrated experience building automations—whether through native platform triggers/rules engines, or tools like Zapier or Make—to solve real workflow problems for customers.

- Comfortable working with API integrations and webhooks at a conceptual and practical level; you don't need to write code, but you need to understand how data flows between systems and be able to configure and troubleshoot integrations.

- Experience working remotely with international teams, with strong written and spoken English suitable for customer calls and training.

- Demonstrated project management skills: managing timelines, stakeholders, and risks across multiple accounts.

- Excellent virtual facilitation skills; able to run effective remote trainings and working sessions with both technical and non-technical users.

- Strong documentation habits and attention to detail – you leave behind clear records of what was configured, why, and how

- Ability to work a schedule that overlaps at least 4–6 hours with US business hours (e.g., PH late afternoon/evening).?

- Reliable high?speed internet connection and a professional, quiet workspace suitable for frequent video calls and screen?sharing.



Nice-to-haves:

- Experience working with customers in at least one of our core verticals: facilities/property management, manufacturing/industrial operations, healthcare field services, or hospitality/food & beverage. Bonus for more than one.

- Experience with maintenance, CMMS, or field operations software. 

- Familiarity with Salesforce, Zendesk, Intercom, or Avoma.

- Experience in a startup or high-change environment where processes are still being defined.

- Familiarity with workflow tools, ticketing systems, or low/no-code configuration platforms.?



What we're NOT looking for: This role is not a fit if you're primarily a support specialist, account manager, or project coordinator who happens to have touched SaaS tools. We need someone who has actually built things—configured complex workflows, designed automations, and solved real operational problems for customers using software. If you can't speak concretely to workflow designs you've built and the customer problems they solved, this likely isn't the right role.



Coast is actively seeking to diversify the team, and you can learn more about us here. Women, minorities, and individuals with disabilities are encouraged to apply. We recognize that not only does a range of experiences and backgrounds help make this team strong, but it also helps us empathize and build better products for our diverse customer base. We are an equal opportunity employer and strive to create a joyful and supportive place to work.



We are not able to support work visas at this time.



TRAINING, ONBOARDING, AND PROBATIONARY PERIOD

We invest heavily in setting you up to succeed. Here's what your first 90 days look like:



Weeks 1–4: Core Training - You'll go through a structured four-week training program combining self-paced learning with live sessions and regular group check-ins with your cohort. By the end, you'll have a strong foundation in Coast's platform, our customer base, and how we work.



Weeks 5–12: Job Training - Training wheels come off gradually. You'll move into hands-on job training where you begin managing your own implementations under guidance — learning by doing with real customers and real stakes.



90-Day Probation Period - Your first 90 days serve as a mutual evaluation period. We want to make sure this is the right fit for you as much as for us, and we'll give you clear expectations, regular feedback, and the support you need to hit the ground running. Successful completion leads to a longer-term engagement with Coast.



WHAT WE CAN OFFER YOU 

The Startup Experience: Shaping Our Growth: Joining Coast at this pivotal stage puts you at the heart of our company’s and product’s evolution. The workplace is energetic, creative, and fast-moving, giving you the chance to help define processes and standards while collaborating directly with founders and teammates who value bold thinking and active input. Here, tea
mbers aren’t just part of the journey—they shape it, building and innovating with visible impact every single day.



Team: We are a tight-knit and very strong core team of ~40 people. We are a highly customer-focused, honest, skilled, direct, solution-oriented, creative, ambitious, and fun crew. Previously, we’ve worked at big companies like Adobe, Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft, and at both early and late stage for startups like 
, HotelTonight, UpKeep, Latch, and Fitbit. We solve tough customer problems together and continually help one another level up.



Product: The market we are going after is massive and way underserved. Our customers love the product and use it for hours every day. What you will be building will directly impact their everyday life. We use Coast as our central nervous system, just like our customers!



CONTRACT DETAILS 

- Engagement type: Independent contractor based in the Philippines; taxes and statutory contributions, and health benefits are the responsibility of the contractor or employer of record.

- Location: Fully remote.

- Compensation: Base pay of $20,000 - $30,000 USD annually as a full time independent contractor. 

- Payment currency and method: USD via bank transfer, paid monthly.

- Working hours: Standard working hours window or overlap expectations, as mentioned above.

- Start date: Late May
ABOUT COAST



Coast is a no-code maintenance and work order platform built specifically for the deskless workforce. Available on Android, iOS, and web, our software is designed for teams on the move—making it easy to stay connected and on top of daily operations, no matter where work happens.



By combining real-time chat with customizable maintenance workflows, Coast helps teams streamline communication, coordinate tasks, and get more done with less friction.



While Coast is flexible enough to support a wide variety of operational needs, our current focus is on solving critical maintenance challenges for organizations that rely on speed, clarity, and accountability.

title: Social Media Video Editor
description:
I’m looking for a skilled video editor who specializes in short-form content for platforms like TikTok, Instagram Reels, and
.



This role is focused on editing engaging, fast-paced content that is designed to perform well on social media, not just look good.



You’ll be working with raw footage from a variety of clients and industries, including restaurants, real estate, and service-based businesses. Each project may have a different style, so the ability to adapt and match different brand tones is important.



I’m specifically looking for someone who understands how to create high-performing content, including viral-style real estate videos using smooth transitions, speed ramping, and engaging visual effects.



What I’m looking for:



Experience editing short-form social media content

Strong understanding of pacing, hooks, and viewer retention

Ability to add captions and on-screen text that enhance the video

Ability to adjust editing style depending on the client/industry

Experience or familiarity with viral-style real estate video editing (transitions, speed ramps, effects)

Familiarity with current social media trends

Ability to follow brand direction and feedback



Bonus if you:



Have experience editing for multiple industries (restaurants, real estate, local businesses, etc.)

Can suggest creative ideas or improvements based on trends as well as conduct market research.



This will be ongoing work for the right person. I’m looking for someone reliable, communicative, and consistent. I want a person who will genuinely want to work with my company and I want someone who is truthful and just willing to keep growing with my business.

title: SENIOR VA — EU B2B, MARKETING & OPERATIONS
description:
SENIOR VIRTUAL ASSISTANT — B2B MARKETING & OPERATIONS

ShoulderSink ApS | Remote | Part-time (50%) with path to full-time by end of 2026

 

 

ABOUT US

 

ShoulderSink is a small Danish B2B company. We make mobile hand hygiene solutions for commercial vehicles — our product is installed in 50,000+ vehicles across Europe. Small team, clear strategy and growing fast across key European markets.

 

 

THE ROLE

 

You work directly with the founder across three areas:

 

Operations (25%): Track our plans, flag slipping deadlines, ask the right questions, keep things moving without being asked.

 

Marketing (35%): Build the monthly content calendar. Draft posts using AI tools. Schedule and publish on LinkedIn,
 and Instagram across several languages. Track META ad campaigns in Germany and the Netherlands.

 

Projects (40%): Build prospect lists, execute approved
 outreach, log everything in Pipedrive CRM, support influencer programmes and event preparation.

 

This is a close working relationship. You will have daily contact with the founder and a weekly video status meeting. You need a minimum 2-hour overlap with European/UK business hours (CET).

 

 

WHAT WE NEED

 

- C2 written English — non-negotiable. German or French would be a plus.

- 3+ years as a VA or coordinator in a B2B environment

- Comfortable using AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT or similar) to draft and refine content

- Experience with LinkedIn,
, Google Sheets and Gmail

- CRM experience — Pipedrive preferred

- Proactive communication style: you flag problems before they become crises, not after

- Available for daily contact and weekly video check-in during EU/UK business hours

 

 

WHAT WE OFFER

 

- $600/month at 50% hours

- Performance bonus after 6 months — based on personal and company KPIs

- Possibility of full-time position by end of 2026 for the right person

- Direct, daily access to the founder — fast decisions, no corporate layers

- A real senior role with genuine responsibility — not a task list

- Stable company, clear processes, documented strategy

- Confidentiality agreement required before onboarding

 

 

PLEASE APPLY VIA THIS PLATFORM

 

Please include:

- A short cover note (max 150 words) — why this role, why you. Be specific. Generic applications will not be considered.

- One concrete example of a time you flagged a problem proactively before it became an issue

- Which AI tools have you used, and for what tasks? Give one specific example.

- Your CRM experience — which platform, and what was your role?

- Your available hours and CET overlap

- Your personal situation and workspace setup

title: Bookkeeper/Tax Preparer
description:
We are currently seeking an experienced and detail oriented part time bookkeeper to join our growing team. This role is ideal for someone who understands the full cycle of bookkeeping, is highly proficient in QuickBooks Online, and has a strong grasp of the US tax system.



Responsibilities:

• Maintain accurate financial records using QuickBooks Online

• Categorize transactions and perform monthly bank and credit card reconciliations

• Generate and analyze financial reports including Profit and Loss, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow statements

• Prepare and maintain clean books for tax preparation

• Identify discrepancies and ensure accuracy across all accounts

• Assist with cleanup and catch up bookkeeping when needed

• Support tax preparation process by organizing financials and working alongside tax preparer

• Assist with input and review of tax returns using Lacerte



Required Qualifications:

• Strong experience with QuickBooks Online

• Ability to generate and interpret cash flow reports

• Solid understanding of accounting principles and bookkeeping workflows

• Knowledge of US tax system, especially as it relates to small businesses and S Corporations

• Experience with Lacerte or similar tax software is a strong plus

• High attention to detail and accuracy

• Ability to work independently and meet deadlines



Preferred Skills:

• Experience with multi entity bookkeeping

• Familiarity with payroll systems such as Gusto

• Understanding of sales tax and local tax requirements

• Ability to set up and optimize QuickBooks Online accounts including chart of accounts and automation rules

• Experience with financial forecasting and budgeting

• Knowledge of handling loan accounts, fixed assets, and depreciation tracking

• Experience preparing books for year end and CPA or tax filing handoff



Position Details:

• Part time position

• Flexible hours

• Remote work available

• Opportunity for long term growth



If you are someone who takes pride in clean and accurate books and enjoys working in a fast paced environment, we would love to hear from you.



To apply, start your message with the words "QuickBooks" and include a short Loom video explaining your QuickBooks experience.

title: PROJECT MANAGER
description:
JOB POSTING · ONLINEJOBS.PH

Business Operations & Lead Generation Specialist

ISAAC'S INDUSTRIES LLC · NAPLES / MIAMI, FLORIDA · REMOTE

$500 / Month

Full Time · 40 hrs/week

100% Remote

Philippines Preferred

Long-Term Role

Who this is for: A sharp, self-directed operator who thrives without hand-holding, loves building systems from scratch, and gets results without being asked twice. You will be the engine that keeps this business moving while the CEO is generating revenue on the front lines.

About Isaac's Industries



Isaac's Industries is a certified minority and women-owned general contracting company based in South Florida operating in commercial construction, post-construction cleaning, demolition, and government procurement. We hold 11 active federal certifications including WOSB, EDWOSB, MBE, SDB, and WBE, and are actively expanding into Miami-Dade County government contracts and private commercial projects.



We are in active growth mode. The CEO is a licensed General Contractor (CGC
) and ICU nurse who runs this business with high standards, clear goals, and zero tolerance for mediocrity. This role exists because growth requires a strong operational backbone — and that's you.



What You Will Own



LinkedIn Lead Generation & Outreach



Identify and connect with Project Managers, General Contractors, Commercial Developers, and Procurement Officers across Florida on LinkedIn daily

Send personalized connection requests and follow-up messages on behalf of the CEO

Build and maintain a warm outreach pipeline of qualified contacts

Track all outreach activity and responses in the CRM

Business Systems & Operations



Build and manage a CRM pipeline (Notion, Airtable, or similar) tracking all leads, proposals, certifications, and follow-ups

Create and document standard operating procedures for recurring business tasks

Manage certification renewal timelines across 11+ federal and state designations

Submit government vendor registration applications and supporting documents

Track all deadlines, renewals, and required actions — nothing falls through the cracks

Administrative & Document Management



Handle government and vendor portal submissions (
, Miami-Dade, state portals)

Organize and maintain all business documents in Google Drive with clear filing systems

Draft outreach
s, proposal cover letters, and follow-up communications for CEO review

Manage CEO inbox and flag priority items daily

Research RFPs, bid opportunities, and government procurement postings

Reporting



Send a concise daily report every evening covering: tasks completed, leads contacted, follow-ups pending, blockers, and next-day plan

Weekly pipeline summary with metrics — outreach volume, response rate, active opportunities

Who You Are



Autonomous — you figure things out, you don't wait to be told what to do next

Systems-minded — you naturally build structure, checklists, and processes wherever you work

LinkedIn-savvy — you know how to research, connect, and build relationships on the platform professionally

Detail-oriented — government documents and certification submissions require precision; errors cost real money

Excellent written English — your outreach messages and
s represent a professional brand

Reliable — you show up, you deliver, you communicate proactively when something comes up

Fast learner — you do not need construction experience; you need the ability to absorb a new industry quickly and execute

Tools You Should Know (or Learn Fast)



Google Workspace — Gmail, Drive, Docs, Sheets

LinkedIn — outreach, Sales Navigator a plus

Notion or Airtable — CRM and project management


 and government vendor portals — will train

Canva — basic use for content support

What success looks like in 30 days: CRM is built and active. Miami-Dade vendor registration submitted. LinkedIn outreach running daily with 20+ new connections per week to target contacts. Daily reports delivered consistently. CEO can see the pipeline and trust the backend is handled.

Compensation & Structure



$500/month — paid monthly via Wise or similar

Full time · approximately 40 hours per week

100% remote — work from anywhere

Start with a 2-week paid trial at $250 before full monthly rate begins

Long-term opportunity for the right person — we grow together

Performance-based increases possible as the business scales

How to Apply



Send your application to [your
] with the subject line: "Isaac's Industries — Operations Specialist"



Your application must include:



A 3–5 sentence introduction — who you are, what makes you the right fit, and one example of a system you built for a previous client or employer

Your resume or work history — relevant experience only, no need to pad it

One sample LinkedIn outreach message you would send to a General Contractor on behalf of Isaac's Industries introducing our services

Your availability — hours per day, timezone, and when you can start

Applications without the LinkedIn sample message will not be reviewed. This is intentional — it tells us immediately whether you can represent this brand professionally.

title: Creative Strategist
description:
We're hiring a Creative Strategist for several DTC/E-Commerce brands.

Full-time | US hours | Permanent Work from Home



This is NOT a general copywriting role.

We’re looking for someone with real direct-response thinking who can help drive better-performing ad creative.



What You'll Be Doing

>Write hooks, angles, and scripts for paid social video ads

>Turn product and customer insights into direct-response ad concepts

>Brief editors / creators clearly so execution is strong

>Help analyze what is or isn’t working in creative performance

>Push better iterations and new ideas across Meta / IG / TikTok, and other platforms



What We're Looking For

>5+ years solid experience creating direct-response video ads for paid social and organic

>Handled  eCommerce / DTC brands

>Meta-first performance creative background, TikTok / UGC-style ad experience

>Worked with editors, creators, UGC managers or media buyers

>Familiar with performance metrics and iteration





To apply, send you updatedr CV, expected rate, earliest start date, and answers to the questions below.

1. How many years of experience do you have creating direct-response video ads for Meta / Instagram / TikTok, specifically hooks, angles, concepts, and scripts?



2. What types of eCommerce / DTC brands or products have you done this for? Describe 1-2 examples.



3. Have you personally written scripts or creative briefs for paid social video ads? If yes, what exactly did you own from research to execution?



4. Walk us through one ad or campaign you worked on that performed well. What was the product, hook, angle, your role, and the result?



5. When an ad underperforms, how do you usually figure out whether the issue is the hook, script/body, offer, creator delivery, edit, or landing page?

title: Executive Admin
description:
APPLY HERE: https://apply.blueprintworkforce.com/apply/



Blueprint Workforce is a U.S.-based workforce partner for construction, design, and trade businesses. We don't place generic virtual assistants. We place specialists into defined roles with structured onboarding, clear SOPs, and ongoing support.

We're hiring an Executive Admin to support a U.S.-based executive in the construction or design industry.

This is not a task-by-task VA role. You'll own the executive's calendar, inbox, task management, and daily operations. You'll anticipate what's needed before being asked. You'll run systems, not wait for instructions.



WHAT YOU'LL DO:



-Manage executive calendars, scheduling, and meeting coordination across time zones

-Own inbox management: triage, draft responses, flag priorities, follow up on pending items

-Build and maintain SOPs, templates, and operational workflows

-Coordinate tasks and deadlines across teams using project management tools

-Prepare documents, reports, presentations, and meeting agendas

-Handle travel coordination, expense tracking, and vendor communication

-Serve as the executive's operational right hand; keep things moving without being told



TOOLS YOU SHOULD KNOW (some or all):



-Google Workspace (Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets)

-Microsoft 365 (Outlook, Word, Excel, Teams)

-Project/Task Management: Asana, 
, Trello, or Notion

-Communication: Slack, Zoom, Calendly

-CRM platforms (HubSpot, Salesforce, or similar)

-QuickBooks (light bookkeeping tasks; not full accounting)



WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR:



-2+ years of experience as an Executive Assistant, Admin, or similar role supporting a senior leader

-Strong written and verbal English communication

-Construction or design industry experience is a strong plus (not required)

-You take ownership. You don't wait to be told what to do next.

-You're organized, detail-oriented, and consistent

-You handle confidential information with discretion

-You're comfortable working U.S. business hours (aligned with your client's time zone)

-Stable internet connection and a professional remote work setup



WHAT BLUEPRINT PROVIDES:



-Full-time, long-term placement (this is not project-based or seasonal)

-Structured 48-hour onboarding with role-specific training and SOPs

-Dedicated support specialist for your first 90 days

-Regular check-ins: Day 3, Day 30, Day 60, Day 90, then quarterly

-Payroll processed through Gusto (direct deposit)

-10 days PTO annually (after 90-day probation) + 5 sick days (available immediately)

-A company that invests in your success, not just your output



WHY BLUEPRINT WORKFORCE IS DIFFERENT:



Most offshore staffing companies hand you a job title and disappear. Blueprint doesn't.

Every placement goes through an 81-question behavioral assessment matched across 6 dimensions. You're not just "available"; you're matched to a role where you'll actually perform and grow.

You get structured onboarding, real support, and a team that treats you as a professional, not a commodity.

We retain 98% of our placements at 6 months. That's not an a
. It's because we build systems that set you up to succeed.



HOW TO APPLY:

APPLY HERE: https://apply.blueprintworkforce.com/apply/



Complete your application through our platform. The process includes a behavioral assessment that matches you to the right role and the right client. No generic applications. No guessing.



If you're ready to be placed, not just hired, apply now.

https://apply.blueprintworkforce.com/apply/

title: Content Operator / Video Editor (Remote)
description:

?? IMPORTANT — READ FIRST

To make sure you pay attention to detail:

???? Include the word “HARVEST” in the subject line of your application.

Applications without this will NOT be reviewed.



???? About Us (Please Read Carefully)

Harvest AF is not a typical financial firm.

We help business owners and individuals:

Understand their numbers

Reduce unnecessary taxes

Build long-term financial structure

But more importantly…

???? We believe our work is a form of service and stewardship.

Our mission is rooted in our faith in Jesus Christ.

Serve people with integrity

Educate, not manipulate

Provide real value before asking for anything in return

This is impact.

If you are just looking for “another editing job,” this is not for you.

???? You will help us turn real knowledge, real client stories, and real impact into content that reaches thousands of people.



???? 1. Video Content Production (Primary Role)

Flow and pacing

Paid Ads

Social media visuals

Easy to read

Well-structured

Branded consistently

Label content clearly

Help maintain a simple content system



???? Expected Weekly Output

Ongoing design support for course materials



? Who This Is For

You are a great fit if you:

Take pride in clean, professional work

title: Project Estimator
description:
APPLY HERE: 



Blueprint Workforce is a U.S.-based workforce partner for construction, design, and trade businesses. We don't place generic virtual assistants. We place specialists into defined roles with structured onboarding, clear SOPs, and ongoing support.

We're hiring a Project Estimator to build takeoffs, cost estimates, and bid packages for U.S.-based general contractors, specialty contractors, and design-build firms.



This is a numbers role. You'll quantify materials, calculate labor, price assemblies, and build the estimates that determine whether your client wins work and protects margins. Accuracy and speed are both non-negotiable.



WHAT YOU'LL DO:



Perform material takeoffs from construction drawings, plans, and specifications

Build detailed cost estimates for labor, materials, equipment, and subcontractor scopes

Prepare bid packages, proposals, and estimate summaries for submission

Analyze subcontractor and vendor quotes for completeness and competitiveness

Maintain and update cost databases, unit pricing, and historical project data

Review plans and specs to identify scope gaps, exclusions, and risk items

Coordinate with project managers, superintendents, and vendors during the estimating process

Support preconstruction planning with budget estimates and value engineering options



TOOLS YOU SHOULD KNOW (some or all):



-PlanSwift

-Bluebeam Revu

-RSMeans or similar cost data references

-Excel/Google Sheets (advanced: formulas, pivot tables, cost modeling)

-Buildertrend or similar construction management platforms



WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR:



-2+ years of experience in construction estimating, quantity surveying, or preconstruction support

-Strong proficiency in digital takeoff tools (PlanSwift, Bluebeam, or similar)

-Demonstrated ability to read and interpret construction drawings and specifications

-Understanding of U.S. construction methods, CSI divisions, and trade scopes

-Strong math and analytical skills; you catch errors before they become expensive

-Experience estimating residential, commercial, or specialty trade projects

-Strong written English communication for coordinating with U.S.-based teams

-You're detail-oriented, deadline-driven, and can manage multiple bids simultaneously

-You're comfortable working U.S. business hours (aligned with your client's time zone)

-Stable internet connection and a professional remote work setup



WHAT BLUEPRINT PROVIDES:



-Full-time, long-term placement (this is not project-based or seasonal)

-Structured 48-hour onboarding with role-specific training and SOPs

-Dedicated support specialist for your first 90 days

-Regular check-ins: Day 3, Day 30, Day 60, Day 90, then quarterly

-Payroll processed through Gusto (direct deposit)

-10 days PTO annually (after 90-day probation) + 5 sick days (available immediately)

-A company that invests in your success, not just your output



WHY BLUEPRINT IS DIFFERENT:



-Most offshore staffing companies hand you a job title and disappear. Blueprint doesn't.

-Every placement goes through an 81-question behavioral assessment matched across 6 dimensions. You're not just "available"; you're matched to a role where you'll actually perform and grow.

-You get structured onboarding, real support, and a team that treats you as a professional, not a commodity.

-We retain 98% of our placements at 6 months. That's not an a
. It's because we build systems that set you up to succeed.



HOW TO APPLY:



APPLY HERE: 



Complete your application through our platform. The process includes a behavioral assessment that matches you to the right role and the right client. No generic applications. No guessing.



If you're ready to be placed, not just hired, apply now.

title: Creative Growth Producer (Short-Form Editor + Ad Research + Basic Copy)
description:
I need one creative who can consistently produce short-form ads and research winning angles. You’ll support car detailing now and ecom product testing later. Your output is the fuel for scaling.



Weekly Deliverables



* 3–6 ad-ready videos (9:16) + 2–4 variations of top concepts

* 15–30 competitor ads logged into a swipe file (hook/angle/structure/CTA)

* Hooks + on-screen copy for every ad (first 2 seconds matter most)



Key Responsibilities



* Edit short-form ads from raw footage + ripped clips (retention-first)

* Create multiple hook variants per concept (hook swaps, pacing swaps, CTA swaps)

* Do market research: find competitor ads, extract patterns, propose angles

* Write basic direct-response captions + on-screen text (clear, punchy)

* Keep clean folders and a tracking sheet for versions and outputs



Requirements



* Strong short-form editing skill (CapCut/Premiere/AE)

* Good English writing for hooks/captions

* Organized and consistent output (not “one good edit sometimes”)



To Apply (Follow Instructions Exactly)

Send:



1. Portfolio: best 3–5 short-form ads you edited

2. Your software + weekly capacity (# edits/week)

3. Write 12 hooks:



   * 6 for detailing (dirty interior/pet hair/stains)

   * 6 for ecom (generic but strong testing hooks)

4. Internet speed + backup plan



Paid Test (Shortlisted Only)

Create 1 ad (20–35s) with 2 different hook intros, plus log 10 competitor ads with angle breakdown.

title: Ops + Dispatch Systems Manager (Calendar / CRM / n8n) — Car Detailing
description:
I need one operator to run daily operations so the business stays booked, organized, and consistent without me. You will own dispatching, calendar management, lead follow-up, pipeline hygiene, review generation, and basic automation maintenance (n8n/CRM).



Key Responsibilities



* Respond to leads fast during shift (SMS/email/DM/chat as needed)

* Book appointments accurately (collect full customer + vehicle + address + service details)

* Manage calendar and dispatch techs (assignment, confirmations, reminders, ETAs)

* Handle reschedules/cancellations professionally

* Maintain CRM pipeline stages (no lost leads, clean notes, correct statuses)

* Run post-service follow-up + review requests + reactivation for past customers

* Maintain/adjust core automations (n8n/Zapier/CRM): lead routing, reminders, review flow



Requirements



* Strong English writing (clear, professional)

* Excellent calendar management + coordination

* CRM experience (GoHighLevel style preferred)

* Bonus: n8n experience or automation troubleshooting mindset



To Apply (Follow Instructions Exactly)

Reply with:



1. Your city + internet speed + backup plan

2. Your available hours overlapping Vancouver (PST)

3. Describe dispatch/calendar experience (volume, tools)

4. Describe any automations you’ve built or maintained

5. Write exact messages for:

   A) New lead asking “price + availability”

   B) Tech running 30 mins late

   C) Review request after job completion



Paid Test (Shortlisted Only)

You’ll receive sample leads + a schedule. You’ll dispatch jobs, write messages, and outline a basic automation flow.

title: Cold Caller for Local Service Business
description:
Cold Caller & Appointment Setter – Local Detailing Company (Growth Opportunity)



We’re looking for driven cold callers to contact local businesses and car dealerships to book appointments for our mobile detailing company. This role isn’t just about making calls — you’ll also follow up, move prospects through the pipeline, and have a real path to grow into a higher-paying inbound closer position.



What You’ll Do:

 • Call local businesses and dealerships to introduce our detailing services

 • Book appointments directly into our calendar

 • Follow up with warm leads to close deals and keep the pipeline active

 • Track call outcomes and update lead statuses

 • Offer other services and upgrades as opportunities come up

 • Work closely with our sales team to improve scripts, offers, and closing strategies



What We’re Looking For:

 • Strong, confident phone skills

 • Comfortable cold calling, following up, and handling objections

 • Organized and able to manage your own pipeline

 • Hungry to grow, with the ambition to move into inbound sales and closing

 • Sales or cold calling experience is a plus, but not required if you’re sharp and willing to learn



What’s In It For You:

 • Real opportunity to grow into inbound closing and higher commission roles

 • Flexible schedule, remote work possible

 • Fast-growing company with multiple services you can sell and upsell

 • Performance-based bonuses and growth incentives



If you’ve got energy, persistence, and know how to hold a conversation that gets results, you’ll love this role. Let’s grow together.

title: SDR / Cold Caller Needed – Healthcare (LTPAC / EHR / Remote Patient Monitoring)
description:
Description:



We are looking for an experienced Sales Development Representative (SDR) or cold caller to help us identify and connect with decision-makers in healthcare facilities across the U.S.



Our company offers a Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM) solution that integrates directly with existing cloud-based software platforms and Electronic Health Records (EHRs) used in the senior care, long-term care, and post-acute care (LTPAC) space. Our solution helps eliminate manual errors in entering patient vitals and improves overall care efficiency.



Your Role:



Conduct outbound cold calls to healthcare facilities (nursing homes, assisted living, rehab centers, etc.)

Identify and obtain contact information for key decision-makers (Administrators, Directors of Nursing, IT leaders)

Qualify leads and gather basic information about current systems and interest level

Set appointments or pass qualified leads to our sales team

Maintain accurate notes and updates in our CRM



Ideal Candidate:



Proven experience in cold calling / SDR / lead generation

Experience calling into healthcare facilities (strongly preferred)

Familiarity with EHR, healthcare SaaS, or RPM solutions is a plus

Excellent communication skills and persistence on the phone

Ability to navigate gatekeepers and reach decision-makers

Organized and detail-oriented

Little to no accent. We do not want the call to sound like it is coming from a call center.

Must know our Cold Call Script. We will test you on it.



Deliverables:



Verified contact information for facility administrators and decision-makers

Call activity tracking and reporting

Qualified leads and scheduled meetings

Compensation:



Hourly + performance-based incentives (bonus per qualified lead or booked meeting)

Long-term opportunity for the right candidate



To Apply:

Please include:



A brief summary of your cold calling experience

Any experience in healthcare or SaaS

A sample script or approach you’ve used successfully

Your availability (hours per week)

We’re looking for someone who can start quickly and help us build a strong outbound pipeline.

title: Short-Stay (Airbnb) Virtual Assistant - 10:00am - 6:30pm Monday, Tues; 6:00am - 2:30pm Friday, Saturday, Sunday
description:
About us 



We’re a Melbourne-based, family-run short-stay property management business. We manage high-quality homes and apartments and focus on fast communication, clear systems, and consistently high guest standards. 



Role overview 



This is a time-sensitive operations role. You’ll start with guest messaging and daily support, then progress into owner communications, listing development, onboarding processes, and cleaning team management. As you become confident in our systems, the role expands into bookkeeping support and new business development support. 



Hours (Phillipines Time): 

10:00am - 6:30pm Monday, Tues

6:00am - 2:30pm Friday, Saturday, Sunday



Key responsibilities (phased) 



Phase 1 - Guest messaging & daily operations 



Respond to guest messages across 
/VRBO (pre-stay, in-stay, and post-stay) 



Handle common requests: early check-in/late check-out, parking, access issues, date changes (per policy), lost items, and general questions 



Escalate urgent issues quickly (e.g., lockouts, safety concerns, urgent maintenance, noise complaints) following our SOPs 



Send check-in/check-out instructions and ensure guests have clear access details 



Maintain accurate notes and task updates in our systems 



Phase 2 - Owner messaging, listings, onboarding & cleaning team management 



Owner communication 



Reply to owner enquiries and keep owners updated on performance, maintenance, and next steps 



Request approvals for quotes and improvements, and ensure decisions are documented 



 



Listing development 



Draft and improve listing titles, descriptions, and key selling points 



Update amenities, house rules, guidebooks, and check-in information for accuracy 



Coordinate photography tasks and ensure listings meet platform standards 



Support pricing and calendar rule updates (training provided) 



 



Onboarding processes 



Follow our onboarding checklist from new lead to go-live 



Collect required property information (access, Wi?Fi, parking, rubbish, linen counts, manuals, etc.) 



Build and maintain house manuals, checklists, and SOPs using our templates 



 



Cleaning team management 



Schedule cleans based on bookings and communicate changes promptly 



Track cleaner reports, quality issues, linen changes, and restocking requirements 



Escalate and resolve cleaning standards issues using our internal process 



Phase 3 - Bookkeeping & business growth 



Assist with bookkeeping admin: invoice tracking, receipt organisation, and basic reconciliations (training provided) 



Support owner statements and tracking of reimbursable expenses 



Help with new business development: lead follow-up, pipeline tracking, proposal packs, and basic research 



Maintain internal trackers and ensure tasks progress to completion 



Tools & systems (nice to have / willingness to learn) 



Airbnb, 
, VRBO (and/or a channel manager such as Hostaway, Guesty, or Lodgify) 



Notion, Dropbox,
 



WhatsApp for team coordination 



Google Sheets / Excel 



Canva or basic design skills are a bonus 



What we’re looking for 



Excellent written English and a warm, professional tone 



Strong attention to detail and ability to follow SOPs 



Calm under pressure with good judgement and clear escalation decisions 



Highly reliable during rostered hours  



Proactive problem-solving and strong task ownership 



Performance indicators 



Fast response times and consistently high-quality messaging 



Constant 5 star guest reviews 



Minimal booking errors and no missed guest messages 



Accurate cleaner scheduling and clear handovers 



Clean, complete onboarding outputs with no gaps 



Clear internal notes and task updates

title: Facebook pages clean-up & Meta Business Manager Setup
description:
Hi there,



I run a very small marketing and communications business in Melbourne, and I’m looking for a detail-oriented VA to help clean up and 
 my
 Page access and permissions.



Over time, I’
 been added to multiple
 Pages (clients, past projects, community groups), and it’s become messy and hard to manage.



I’d like to centralise everything into my Meta Business Manager and remove any unnecessary or outdated access.



What You’ll Be Doing:



1. Audit & Organisation



Review all
 Pages I currently have access to

Categorise pages into:

Active clients (keep)

Inactive / no longer needed (remove)

Personal/irrelevant



2. Meta Business Manager Setup



Move or connect active pages into my Business Manager

Ensure correct roles and permissions are set (admin/editor etc.)

Remove duplicate or confusing access (personal vs Business Manager)



3. Clean-Up & Access Removal



Remove me from pages that are no longer relevant

Where needed, assist in contacting page owners/admins with instructions



4. Simple Documentation



Create and maintain a Google Sheet tracking:

Page name

Status (active/inactive)

Access type

Actions taken



5. Risk Awareness



Make sure no pages lose admin access during changes

Flag anything unclear before making changes



Requirements:

Strong experience with
 Pages + Meta Business Manager

You understand page roles, permissions, and access levels

High attention to detail (this is clean-up work, not creative work)

Good written English (for occasional messages to page owners)

Comfortable working independently and asking questions when needed



Nice to Have (but not essential):

Experience managing multiple client accounts

Familiarity with agencies or social media workflows



Hours & Timeline:

Initial project: approx. 5–10 hours



How to Apply:



Please include:



A short summary of your experience with Meta Business Manager

An example of a similar task you’ve done (if any)

Your hourly rate in $AUD

The word “Ripple” at the top of your application so I know you’ve read this



Bonus (optional):



If you’ve ever cleaned up a messy Business Manager before, tell me what the biggest issue was and how you fixed it.



I look forward to receiving your submission or questions. 

Thanks,

Kate

title: Website Builder + Deployment VA (AI-Assisted, Long-Term Role)
description:
Hi! I run a small US-based web design and hosting company that builds simple, clean websites for local small businesses (restaurants, salons, contractors, etc.). I'm looking for one reliable person to join the team long-term and handle the full build + deploy process.



**What you'll do**

- Build small business websites using AI tools (Claude, Base44, Lovable, or similar — I'll show you the workflow)

- Deploy them to hosting (Vercel / Netlify / similar)

- Connect client domains (Cloudflare, GoDaddy, Namecheap DNS setup)

- Make small edits when clients request them



**About the work**

- Most sites take 45–90 minutes once you know the system

- You'll get client info from their existing website or Google Business Listing

- Some sites are "spec" (built before the client signs) and some are for confirmed paying clients

- Moderate volume — I do active sales outreach weekly



**Pay structure**

- **$10 per spec site built** (paid weekly)

- **$100 bonus per site that converts to a paying client**

- Potential monthly retainer down the line for ongoing support once we hit volume



Real math: if you build 20 spec sites a week and 2 convert, that's $200 + $200 = $400/week. The better the sites look, the higher the conversion, the more you earn.



**Who I'm looking for**

- Good written English

- Comfortable with AI tools 

- Reliable and communicative on WhatsApp

- Detail-oriented — typos and broken links kill conversions

- Available 4–6 hours/day, flexible on timing but some overlap with US evenings (PH morning/midday) preferred

- Long-term mindset — I don't want to re-hire in 3 months



**How to apply**

Message Include the word “Apple” in your subject response. Applications without this will not be reviewed.

1. A short message telling me why this role fits you

2. One example of a website you've built or deployed with AI 

3. Your available hours (PH time)



The first task will be a **paid trial build** — one real spec site, $20 flat, 24-hour turnaround. If it's good, we start working together ongoing.



Looking forward to hearing from you!

title: Premium Executive Assistant (Medical & Legal Ops)
description:
We are a U.S.-based medical practice seeking a highly intelligent, highly organized, reliable Executive Assistant / Operations Coordinator to work directly with the owner and help keep the practice running smoothly.



This is not a beginner virtual assistant role.



You will function as a right hand to leadership and as a quasi-office manager from a remote position. You must be proactive, calm under pressure, organized, discreet, and able to turn messy systems into clean systems.



What you will do

-Organize, clean, and maintain Google Drive and internal documents

-Remove redundancies, create folder structures, naming conventions, and archive rules

-Help prepare and organize payroll-related admin tasks and follow-up

-Draft, update, and maintain documents, forms, spreadsheets, and SOPs

-Track tasks, deadlines, and follow-through so nothing gets missed

-Support scheduling,
, document preparation, and special projects

-Identify inefficiencies and propose better systems

-Help leadership stay organized and focused



What we need

-Strong experience in executive assistance, operations support, or administrative management

-Excellent written English and professional judgment

-Strong Google Workspace skills, especially Google Drive, Docs, Sheets, Gmail, and Calendar. MS Office/Teams/Word

-Outstanding organization and follow-through

-Ability to learn new systems quickly

-Good judgment with confidential information

-Able to work independently without constant hand-holding

-Reliable internet, reliable computer, and a professional remote work setup



Strong pluses

-Payroll, bookkeeping, or financial admin experience

-Medical, clinic, or healthcare admin experience

-Experience cleaning and organizing large cloud-based file systems

-Experience building SOPs and process documentation



This is not the right role for you if

-You disappear when blocked

-You need constant supervision for basic tasks

-You are disorganized with files, deadlines, or follow-up

-You exaggerate your skills

-You are overloaded with other full-time work



Hours and growth



This role starts part-time at 20–25 hours per week and may grow into a larger role for the right person.



How to apply



At the top of your application, write this word:

ORDER



Then include the following:



-Your OnlineJobs profile link

-A short paragraph describing the most complex admin/organization role you have handled

-Your experience with Google Drive cleanup, folder systems, file naming, or document control

-Your experience with payroll, bookkeeping, or financial admin

-Your current work commitments and whether you have other clients/jobs

-Your expected rate and weekly availability

-Your answer to this question:

-You inherit a Google Drive full of duplicate files, poor naming, and no clear structure. What are your first 5 steps?





Only applicants who follow all instructions will be reviewed.

Shortlisted candidates will complete a paid trial task and a video interview.

title: Graphic Designer — Social Media & Paid Ads Creative (Long-Term, Remote)
description:
Graphic Designer — Social Media & Paid Ads Creative

Groe Digital | Remote | Long-Term Position | EST Day Shift



WHO WE ARE



Groe Digital is a paid advertising and lead generation agency. We run Meta 
 & Instagram) ads for local service businesses — gyms, restaurants, painters, salons, real estate, and home services and ecom businesses.



We're building a small, tight team of A-players who own their craft. This is a long-term position with room to grow as we grow.



THE ROLE

We need a graphic designer who can produce scroll-stopping visuals for two purposes:



Organic social media content (Instagram/Facebook posts, Reels covers, Stories, carousels) — 3 graphics per client per month

Paid Meta ad creatives (static images and graphic ads) — volume varies by client budget



You'll work across multiple clients in different industries (fitness, restaurants, home services, beauty, real estate, retail). Each client has a defined brand identity, content pillars, and visual style guide that you'll follow — but we also want your creative eye. We don't want someone who just follows instructions robotically. We want someone who takes a brief and makes it better than what we imagined.



WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING



Designing social media graphics optimized for Instagram and
 (feed posts, carousels, stories, Reel covers)

Designing ad creatives optimized for Meta campaigns (static ads, carousel ads, story ads)



Working from detailed briefs that include the key message, visual direction, brand guidelines, and reference examples

Designing with a social-media-first mindset — every graphic should be designed to stop the scroll, not just look pretty in a portfolio

Using client brand guidelines (colors, fonts, visual style) to maintain consistency while bringing fresh creative ideas

Using existing content and inspiration as starting points — we'll share reference posts, competitor examples, and previous designs to build from

Creating new takes and variations on proven designs — not just copying, but evolving

Delivering editable source files (Canva links preferred for team access, or PSD/AI if complex)

Delivering exported files in correct dimensions per platform

Communicating clearly on WhatsApp about timelines, questions, and delivery

Taking revision feedback professionally and delivering fixes within 24 hours



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Must-haves:



Strong portfolio showing social media graphics AND ad creatives (not just logos or print)

Experience designing specifically for Instagram and
 (you understand what performs, not just what looks good)

Proficiency in Adobe Photoshop

Proficiency in Adobe Illustrator

Proficiency in Canva (we use this for team collaboration and editable templates)

Experience with AI design tools (Midjourney, Adobe Firefly, Leonardo, or similar) for generating concepts, backgrounds, or elements — you should be comfortable using AI as part of your workflow, not afraid of it

Ability to maintain brand consistency across multiple clients simultaneously

Clean, modern design aesthetic — we work with local businesses, not Fortune 500 corporations, so designs should feel approachable and human, not overly corporate

Reliable internet connection and ability to meet deadlines consistently



Strong pluses:



Experience designing for Meta/Facebook ad campaigns specifically (you understand ad creative best practices — bold text, clear CTA, mobile-first)

Experience working with a marketing agency (managing multiple client brands)

Motion graphics basics (simple animations for Stories or Reels)

Photo editing and retouching skills

Experience with Figma

Understanding of basic marketing principles (what makes someone click an ad vs scroll past)



HOW WE WORK



We communicate via WhatsApp (async, not calls)

Every graphic comes with a detailed brief: client name, platform, dimensions, key message, visual direction, reference examples, and a link to the client's brand identity document

Each brief is tagged "FOR SOCIAL" (organic posts) or "FOR ADS" (Meta campaigns) so you know the destination

Briefs are batched monthly per client — you'll receive all of a client's briefs at once, not drip-fed one at a time

Standard turnaround: 48 hours per graphic

Revision turnaround: 24 hours

You deliver to a shared Google Drive folder, organized per client

Weekly 15-minute check-in call with your team lead

We value speed, consistency, and communication. If something is unclear in a brief, ask BEFORE you start — don't guess



WHAT WE PROVIDE



Detailed brand identity documents for every client (colors, fonts, voice, visual style, content pillars)

Clear briefs with references and direction for every graphic

Organized Drive folder per client where you pick up briefs and deliver finished work

Consistent workload — this is not feast-or-famine freelance work. You'll have steady clients every month

Room to grow — as we add clients, your hours (and pay) can increase

Creative freedom within brand guidelines — we want your ideas, not just execution

Professional, respectful working relationship — we treat designers as skilled professionals, not pixel pushers



WHAT THE FIRST 2 WEEKS LOOK LIKE (Paid Trial)

Week 1: You receive brand identity docs for 2-3 clients. You study their brands. You receive your first batch of briefs (2-3 graphics). You deliver, we review, we give feedback.

Week 2: Full workflow — you receive a full month's briefs for multiple clients. You deliver on schedule. We evaluate speed, quality, communication, and brand consistency.

If Week 1-2 go well, you're on the team long-term.

TO APPLY

Please send:



Your portfolio — a link showing social media graphics and/or ad creatives you've designed. We want to see work that was made for Instagram/Facebook, not just logos or print design. If you've designed Meta ad creatives, highlight those.

A short Loom video (2-3 minutes, with your face on camera) walking through 2-3 of your best social media designs. Show us the work and explain your design thinking — why you chose those colors, that layout, that font. This tells us how you think, not just what you can make.

Your hourly rate and availability (hours per week you can commit).

Tools you use daily (Photoshop, Illustrator, Canva, AI tools — which ones specifically).

Your experience with Meta/Facebook ad creative (even if limited — be honest).



Applications without a portfolio link will not be reviewed.

title: Consulting Controller / Jr. CFO
description:
We are seeking a highly capable Consulting Controller / Junior CFO to join our growing firm. This is a full-time, client-facing role focused on delivering high-level accounting oversight, financial reporting, and strategic financial guidance across a diverse portfolio of small to mid-sized businesses.



You will operate as the financial backbone for multiple clients—owning the numbers, driving clarity, and supporting decision-making.



Key Responsibilities



Manage the full accounting cycle across multiple client engagements

Oversee and review bookkeeping to ensure accuracy and completeness

Prepare and analyze monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements

Lead budgeting, forecasting, and scenario modeling efforts

Monitor and manage client cash flow, including forward-looking projections

Identify financial risks, inefficiencies, and opportunities for improvement

Support tax preparation readiness (in coordination with tax team)

Communicate directly with clients in a clear, professional manner

Build and maintain financial dashboards and reporting tools



Required Qualifications



CPA designation (required)

Minimum 10 years of progressive accounting/finance experience

Demonstrated success managing the full accounting cycle for US firms

Strong experience with:

Financial reporting

Budgeting and forecasting

Cash flow management

Advanced Microsoft Excel skills, including:

Macros

Pivot tables

Experience working with QuickBooks Online or similar cloud accounting platforms

Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving ability

Excellent written and verbal communication skills



Preferred (Nice to Have)



Experience supporting CAN,  AUS, or UK clients

Familiarity with cross-border accounting/tax considerations

Prior experience in a consulting or multi-client environment

Background in industries such as  e-commerce, construction, SaaS, or professional services



Work Environment



Collaborative, fast-paced, and client-focused

Opportunity to work directly with firm leadership and clients



Compensation



USD 12 - 20 per hour, depending on experience and qualifications

Full-time workload with consistent hours

Performance-based growth opportunities



Why Join BSP



Exposure to international and cross-border engagements

Opportunity to operate at a Controller / CFO level—not just transactional accounting

Work with a firm focused on advisory, not just compliance

Clear path for growth and increased responsibility



When you Apply



Please submit:



Your resume

A brief summary of your experience managing the full accounting cycle

Examples of financial reporting or modeling work (if available)

Confirmation of CPA designation

title: GL Wizard Wanted: Help Us Clean Up & Scale Our US Apartment Business! (Part-Time)
description:
Real Estate Accountant & "GL Wizard" (Part-Time / Remote) ???????????

•	The Commitment: Part-Time (Up to 25 hours/week)

•	The Vibe: Fast-paced, high-growth US Apartment Business

•	The Hours: Graveyard Shift (PHT) – Join us while the US is wide awake!

•	The Location: Your couch, your home office, or your favorite café (Remote)

________________________________________

Let’s be real: Accounting is usually boring. We aren't. We are a growing US-based apartment business, and we’re moving fast. We don't have a "accounting department" filled with cubicles and dusty files. Instead, we have a lean, modern team that values transparency, tech, and getting things right the first time.

We aren’t looking for a "data entry clerk." We’re looking for a Real Estate Accounting Pro who actually enjoys the "detective work" of cleaning up a messy General Ledger. If you get a hit of dopamine from seeing a balance sheet finally hit zero, you’re our person.

What You’ll Actually Be Doing

You’ll be the "Source of Truth" for our property financials. No fluff, just high-impact work:

•	The Great Cleanup: We have some historical "accounting cobwebs" that need a pro to sweep through. You’ll dive into the GL and fix discrepancies like a boss.

•	Apartment Life: You’ll handle the stuff that makes an apartment business tick—security deposits, rent rolls, mortgage escrows, and CapEx.

•	Tech Stack Mastery: You’ll live inside AppFolio, Yardi, or ResMan and use Excel to make the data dance.

•	Investor-Ready Books: You’ll own the month-end close so that when we look at our reports, we know the numbers are 100% solid.

________________________________________

Are You "The One"? (Must-Haves)

•	Multifamily Experience is a MUST: You’ve worked with apartment complexes before. You know that a "Tenant Ledger" isn't the same thing as a "General Ledger."

•	The Software: You are a ninja in AppFolio, Yardi, or ResMan.

•	Experience: You’ve got 4+ years under your belt. You’re experienced enough to work without a boss breathing down your neck.

•	The Night Owl Life: You’re cool with the Graveyard Shift. You’ll be syncing up with our US team in real-time.

•	Zero Fluff: You’re meticulous, detail-oriented, and you hate "sloppy" books more than anything.

________________________________________

Why You’ll Love It Here

•	No Micro-management: We don’t care where you work or if you’re wearing a hoodie—we just care that the books are perfect.

•	Growth Potential: We’re expanding. Today it’s 25 hours; tomorrow, who knows?

•	Modern Culture: We’re a 2026-minded company. We use the best tools and value your time.

________________________________________

How to Apply (Don’t be boring!)

We want to see your personality. Send us:

1.	Your Resume (PDF is best).

2.	A quick note telling us:

o	Which property management software is your favorite and why?

o	What’s the most "chaotic" set of books you’ve ever successfully cleaned up?

o	Confirm you're down for the Graveyard Shift (25 hours/week).

3.	Your desired hourly rate (PHP or USD).

4.	The most important question: What is your favorite movie of all time? (Bonus points if it’s a classic).

Let’s build something great together. Apply now!

title: SEO Strategist
description:
SEO Strategist



Hey! We’re Dilate, a result driven digital marketing agency with an impressive, diverse client list and a world-class, global team.

It’s our mission to do great marketing, for great businesses, ethically and in a way that benefits us all. We want our clients to win, their customers to win, and our employees to win.



We’ve got pretty ambitious growth plans and we’re looking for an SEO Strategist to join our team.



We are looking for someone with an in-depth knowledge of SEO, a strong understanding of search intent and algorithms, a keen eye for detail, and a focus on driving measurable growth through organic search. Ideally, you will have experience working agency-side, managing SEO strategies for multiple clients across different industries.



This opportunity is remote working from home, full-time Monday to Friday 9–5 pm AWST (the successful candidate must be available during these hours) as we are an agency located in Perth, Western Australia.



So, the job…

* Develop and execute SEO strategies that align with client goals and deliver measurable organic growth.

* Conduct keyword research, competitor analysis, and technical SEO audits to identify opportunities.

* Optimise on-page SEO elements including content, metadata, internal linking, and site structure.

* Analyse SEO performance using tools such as Google Analytics, Google Search Console, and SEO platforms.

* Monitor rankings, traffic, and conversions, and adjust strategies to improve performance and ROI.

* Stay up to date with SEO trends, algorithm updates, and best practices.

* Provide clear, data-driven insights and strategic recommendations to clients and internal teams.

* Collaborate with content, development, and paid media teams to support integrated marketing strategies.





Your skills:

* Excellent written and spoken English.

* Strong understanding of technical SEO, on-page SEO, and off-page SEO fundamentals.

* Experience using SEO tools such as Google Search Console, Google Analytics, Ahrefs, SEMrush, or similar.

* Proven track record of improving organic traffic, rankings, and conversions.

* Solid understanding of search intent and content optimisation.

* Good instincts for conversion rate optimisation and maximising website performance.

* Ability to translate data into actionable SEO strategies and clear recommendations.

* Strategic thinker with the ability to plan long-term growth while executing tactically.

* Ability to work independently and as part of a team, with a proactive, solutions-focused mindset.

* Hungry for knowledge, learning, and personal development.

* At least 3mbps of stable internet connection (with a backup internet).

* MUST have a mid-level performance desktop or laptop (good for multitasking).

* MUST have a working mic & webcam.



This is a great opportunity to be a part of a fast growing agency.

If you feel that this is the job for you, we would love to hear from you! 

Please include your resume and cover letter.



Thanks for your time,

The Dilate Digital Team

title: Remote Sales Coordinator
description:
Department:

Sales & Marketing / Customer Service

Reports To:

Sales Manager / Commercial Manager

Employment Type: Full-time



Company Overview

Sinotech Marine, headquartered in Hong Kong, is a global maritime technical consulting and technology service company. Founded by senior maritime professionals, Sinotech Marine offers specialized independent technical consulting services worldwide to ship owners, buyers, shipping equity investors, banks, and ship brokers. Our services include vessel condition assessments, investment risk analysis, and vessel performance evaluations. Through the integration of innovative digital technology solutions, we aim to minimize financial and operational risks and optimize fleet performance for our clients globally.



Job Summary

The Sales Coordinator supports the marine sales team in achieving business objectives by managing customer inquiries, preparing quotations and presentations, following up on sales leads, processing orders, and coordinating with suppliers and logistics. This role ensures smooth sales operations, professional communication, and high customer satisfaction in the marine industry.

Key Responsibilities

• Assist the sales team in preparing proposals, quotations, and tender submissions for marine equipment, spare parts, and services.

• Create and design PowerPoint presentations and sales materials for client meetings, product showcases, and management reviews.

• Follow up proactively on sales leads, quotations, and customer inquiries to convert opportunities into sales.

• Coordinate with suppliers, ship chandlers, and technical teams to obtain pricing, lead times, and product information.

• Process sales orders and maintain accurate records in ERP or CRM systems.

• Liaise with logistics and procurement teams to ensure timely delivery of marine supplies and proper documentation.

• Serve as the primary point of contact for customer inquiries and provide timely, professional responses.

• Support account managers in maintaining strong customer relationships and ensuring satisfaction.

• Prepare and maintain sales reports, forecasts, and performance summaries.

• Ensure all documentation related to sales orders, invoices, delivery notes, and certificates is accurate and properly filed.

• Collaborate with technical, operations, and finance teams to resolve order or payment issues.

• Provide administrative support to the sales and marketing teams for events, exhibitions, and client meetings.

• Lead Generation



Qualifications & Skills

Education:

* Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Marine Engineering, Maritime Studies, or a related field.

Experience:

* Minimum 2–4 years of experience in sales coordination, lead management, or customer service, preferably within the marine, shipping, or offshore industry (but not required)

Skills & Competencies:

• Strong communication and interpersonal skills.

• Excellent proficiency in Microsoft Office, especially PowerPoint, Excel, and Word.

• Good understanding of lead management and follow-up processes.

• Knowledge of marine documentation and import/export procedures.

• Ability to multitask, prioritize, and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.

Key Attributes

• Customer-focused and proactive approach.

• Creative and detail-oriented in preparing presentations.

• Strong organizational and problem-solving skills.

• Professionalism, teamwork, and integrity.

title: Virtual Assistant, Sales and Operations (Remote)

Executive support

Assist the founder with scheduling, follow ups, and day to day coordination.



Administrative and financial tracking

Monitor incoming customer payments, organise receipts and invoices, and prepare clear documentation for accounting purposes.



Customer support

This role is best suited for someone with a proven background in sales, business development, or account management who is confident and articulate in English across both video and voice communication. You should be comfortable leading conversations, building trust, and closing opportunities, while also being highly organised and reliable with administrative responsibilities. Experience within e commerce or as a Virtual Assistant is advantageous, particularly familiarity with platforms such as Gorgias or Shopify. The ability to work independently with minimal supervision is essential.



Details

-Remote full time position

-Working hours aligned with Switzerland time (CET/CEST)

-Salary range to be discussed



Application process



Please submit a short Loom or video introduction describing a situation where you successfully persuaded someone to make a purchase. Applications without a video submission will not be considered.

title: Executive Assistant
description:
READ THIS FIRST



[SHORT INTRO OF OUR COMPANY]



We are a Singapore based company.



Our main core business involves coaching and training a new generation of financial advisors to reach out to more people, transform more lives and build the business of their dreams.



We have coached more than 3000 Financial Advisors and have more than 8 years under our belt in doing so.



Our clients are mainly in Singapore, Malaysia and Hong Kong and we are on track to reaching out to more of them as we speak.



Right now, We are looking for an Executive Assistant to be part of our company, and to be working directly with the Chief Executive Officer (CEO).



As the Executive Assistant of our company, you will be responsible for coordinating, planning events, communicating to the team and clients, and organising all the backend logistics and operations of the company.



More importantly, your main objective is to buy back time for the CEO.



Your position will play an important and crucial role in the team’s success.





[HOW LIFE IS GONNA LOOK LIKE FOR YOU]



You will be involved in the day to day communications and supporting the CEO directly, assisting in random tasks or any tasks that the CEO requires.





There is no limit to duties an executive assistant can handle, which is why if you are taking on this role, you must have a confident, problem-solving-can-do attitude.



We will not sugarcoat anything but here’s how it is like as well.



You WILL be in a high pressure environment that will force you to step up and grow as an individual.



There will be no bullshit beautiful lies, but rather ugly truth - although necessary - but ugly all the same that will poise you for growth.



While it might be intense, the rewards are great.



Bonuses, employee benefits and perks that will ensure you get well remunerated for your efforts.



The “work hard, play hard and work even harder mantra” is here.



Because we are going to be poised for crazy growth and expansion, you will have to put on your thinking cap, initiative and out of your comfort zone to get things done.





You'll get to attend world-class business, marketing and personal development programs for FREE and rub elbows with renowned Asian entrepreneurs and Financial Advisors.



You’ll be working with an international team, and you’ll even get to hang out with them at least once a year at our annual retreat in an exotic location.





=========
